title: A Simple Morning
content:
Tom opens his eyes at 7 o’clock.
 The room is quiet. He feels tired.

He gets out of bed and walks to the bathroom.
 He washes his face with cold water.
 Then, he looks in the mirror for a few seconds.

Tom goes to the kitchen.
 He makes a simple breakfast.
 He eats bread and drinks hot coffee.

The coffee smells good.
 Tom feels a little better.
Then, he looks at the clock.

He quickly puts on his jacket and takes his bag.
 Outside, the street is busy.
 Some people walk fast. A bus stops near him.

At 8 o’clock, he arrives at the office.
 He sits down in his chair and takes a deep breath.
questions: What time does Tom wake up?
What does Tom drink for breakfast?
Why does Tom walk fast?
How does Tom feel at the end?
answers_q: He wakes up at 7 o’clock.
He drinks coffee.
He is late.
He feels better.
cloze: He takes his ___ and leaves the house.
speaking: “I’m a little late.”
example: “I’m a little late for work.”

title: Anna at the Supermarket
content:
Anna leaves work at the end of the day.
 She feels a little tired.
“I just need a few things,” she thinks.

The supermarket is bright and busy.
 Anna takes a basket and walks slowly inside.

First, she goes to the fruit section.
 She sees red apples, yellow bananas, and fresh oranges.

She takes some apples and two bananas.
 Then, she walks to the bread section.

Anna smiles and puts one loaf of bread in her basket.
 She also buys milk.

At the checkout, many people are waiting.
 A man looks at his phone.
 A child talks loudly.

Finally, it is her turn.
 She pays for her food and takes her bag.

Outside, the air is cool.
 Anna walks home slowly.
Now, she feels calm and happy.
questions: Where does Anna go after work?
What fruit does she buy?
Why does Anna wait?
How does Anna feel at the end?
answers_q: She goes to the supermarket.
She buys apples and bananas.
There are many people.
She feels happy.
cloze: She waits in ___ at the checkout.
speaking: “I just need a few things.”
example: “I just need a few things from the supermarket.”

title: A Walk in the Park
content:
It is Sunday morning.
 Paul wakes up late and stays in bed for a few minutes.

The apartment is quiet.
 He feels relaxed and happy today.

Paul goes to the kitchen and makes coffee.
 Then, he eats a small breakfast near the window.
“This is a nice morning,” he thinks.
After breakfast, Paul puts on his shoes and goes to the park.

The weather is warm and sunny.
 Many people are outside.

Some people walk with friends.
 Two children play with a ball.
 An old man reads a newspaper on a bench.

Paul walks slowly on the small park path.
 He hears birds and listens to the wind in the trees.
Then, he sees a small brown dog.

The dog runs after a ball and jumps happily.
 Paul smiles.
After some time, Paul sits on a bench and watches people quietly.
One hour later, he stands up and goes home.
questions: How does Paul feel in the morning?
What is the weather like?
What animal does Paul see?
Where does Paul sit?
answers_q: He feels relaxed and happy.
It is sunny and warm.
He sees a dog.
He sits on a bench.
speaking: “It’s a nice morning.”
example: “It’s a nice morning for a walk.”

title: At the Café
content:
Lisa walks through the city in the afternoon.
 The streets are busy, and many people are outside.
She feels a little tired, so she goes into a small café near the corner.

Inside, the café is warm and quiet.
 Soft music is playing.
Lisa sits at a table near the window and takes off her jacket.
A man comes to her with a smile.
“Hello, what do you want?” he asks.
“I want a coffee, please,” she says.
A few minutes later, he brings the coffee to her table.
The coffee is hot and smells very good.
Lisa drinks slowly and looks outside the window.

She sees people walking in the street.
 A woman talks on her phone.
 Two friends laugh together.
questions: Where does Lisa go?
What does Lisa order?
Where does Lisa sit?
How does Lisa feel at the end?
answers_q: She goes to a café.
She orders a coffee.
She sits near the window.
She feels calm and happy.
speaking: “I want a coffee, please.”
example: “I want a coffee, please. Thank you.”

title: My New Job
content:
Mark’s alarm rings at 6:30 in the morning.
 He opens his eyes quickly.
Today is his first day at a new job.
“What if I make a mistake?” he thinks.

Mark gets out of bed and goes to the kitchen.
 He eats toast and drinks hot tea.

Outside, the morning air is cold.
 People wait at the bus stop with their bags and coffee cups.
Mark gets on the bus and looks out the window quietly.
After some time, he arrives at the office building.

The office is big, clean, and very quiet.
 Some people work at their desks.
 Others talk softly near the coffee machine.
Then, his boss comes to him.

He sits at his desk and opens his computer.
 At first, he works slowly, but he is careful and listens closely.
At the end of the day, Mark feels tired but happy.
questions: Why is Mark nervous?
What does he drink at breakfast?
How is the office?
How does Mark feel at the end?
answers_q: It is his first day at work.
He drinks tea.
It is big and quiet.
He feels happy.
cloze: He opens his ___ at his desk.
speaking: “I’m a little nervous.”
example: “I’m a little nervous today.”

title: A Rainy Day
content:
It is Monday morning.
 Emma wakes up and hears the rain outside.
She looks through the window and sighs.

The sky is gray, and the streets are wet.
 Emma does not like rainy days.

She goes to the kitchen and drinks a quick cup of coffee.
 Then, she puts on her coat and takes her black umbrella.
Outside, the air is cold and quiet.

Emma walks slowly to work.
 Water falls on the sidewalk and small cars pass in the street.

There are not many people outside today.
 Most people walk quickly with umbrellas.
When she arrives at work, she takes off her wet coat and sits down at her desk.
Then, she makes a cup of hot tea.
The tea is warm and smells nice.
Now, she feels warm, calm, and comfortable.
questions: What is the weather like?
Why does Emma sigh?
What does Emma take with her?
What does she drink at work?
answers_q: It is rainy.
She does not like rain.
She takes an umbrella.
She drinks hot tea.
speaking: “I don’t like rainy days.”
example: “I don’t like rainy days. They are cold.”

title: My Best Friend
content:
I have a best friend.
 His name is Leo.

Leo is 26 years old, and he lives near my house.
 We know each other very well.
We meet every week, usually on Saturday afternoon.

Leo is always relaxed and smiling.
 He likes to tell funny stories, and he makes people laugh.

We both like music and movies.
 Sometimes, we watch films at my apartment.
 Other times, we go to the cinema together.
On Saturdays, we often go to a small café in the city.

The café is quiet and comfortable.
 We sit near the window and drink coffee.

We talk about many things.
 Sometimes we talk about work.
 Sometimes we talk about music or old memories.
Hours pass quickly when we are together.

Leo is funny, kind, and easy to talk to.
 When I have a bad day, he always listens.
I feel lucky to have a friend like him.
questions: What is the friend’s name?
What do they like?
Where do they go on Saturday?
How is Leo?
answers_q: His name is Leo.
They like music and movies.
They go to a café.
He is funny and kind.
cloze: We drink coffee and ___ a lot.
speaking: “He is funny and kind.”
example: “My best friend is funny and kind.”

title: At the Restaurant
content:
It is evening, and Sarah feels hungry after work.
 The city lights shine outside as she walks down the street.
She goes into a small restaurant near the center of town.

Inside, the restaurant is warm and calm.
 Soft music plays quietly in the background.
Sarah sits at a table near the window and takes off her coat.
A waiter comes to her with a smile.
She looks at the menu for a few minutes.

Everything looks good, but finally she chooses chicken and rice.
 She also orders a glass of water.
A short time later, the waiter brings her meal.
The food smells very good, and the rice is hot.

Sarah starts eating slowly.
 Outside, she watches people walking past the restaurant window.
When she finishes her meal, she pays the bill and thanks the waiter.
Then, she leaves the restaurant happy.
questions: Where does Sarah sit?
What does she order?
How does the food smell?
How does Sarah feel at the end?
answers_q: She sits near the window.
She orders chicken, rice, and water.
It smells very good.
She feels happy.
speaking: “I would like chicken and rice, please.”
example: “I would like chicken and rice, please.”

title: My Evening
content:
After work, I finally go home.
 It is already dark outside, and the streets are quiet.
I feel a little tired after a long day.
When I arrive home, I put my bag near the door and take a hot shower.
The warm water helps me relax.
Then, I change into comfortable clothes and make dinner in the kitchen.
Tonight, I eat pasta and drink a glass of water.

After dinner, I sit on the sofa and turn on the TV.
 I watch a movie for one hour.
The apartment is calm and peaceful.

Later, my phone rings.
 It is my friend.

We talk for a few minutes about our day.
 We laugh together, and I feel better.
At 10:30, I turn off the lights and go to bed.
questions: How does the person feel after work?
What does the person do before dinner?
Where does the person sit to watch TV?
What time does the person go to bed?
answers_q: The person feels tired.
The person takes a shower.
The person sits on the sofa.
The person goes to bed at 10:30.
speaking: “I’m tired. I want to relax.”
example: “I’m tired. I want to relax at home.”

title: A Day at the Beach
content:
It is a beautiful sunny day.
 The sky is blue, and the weather is warm.
Paul and Anna decide to go to the beach together.

In the morning, they prepare a small bag with food and drinks.
 They bring sandwiches, juice, water, and some fruit.
When they arrive, they hear the sound of the sea and the wind.

Many people are already on the beach.
 Some children play in the sand, and others swim in the water.
Paul and Anna find a quiet place near the sea and sit on the warm sand.
“This is nice,” Anna says with a smile.
After a few minutes, Paul runs into the water and swims in the sea.
The water is cool and clear.
Anna walks slowly along the beach and looks at the waves.

Later, they sit together and eat their sandwiches.
 They drink juice, laugh, and talk about their week.
In the evening, the sky becomes orange and pink.
Paul and Anna pack their things and go home tired but happy.
They feel calm and relaxed after their day at the beach.
questions: Where do Paul and Anna go?
What do they bring?
What does Paul do in the sea?
How do they feel at the end?
answers_q: They go to the beach.
They bring food and drinks.
He swims.
They feel happy and relaxed.
speaking: “This is a beautiful day.”
example: “This is a beautiful day at the beach.”

title: At the Bus Stop
content:
It is 7:30 in the morning.
 The streets are still quiet, and the sky is gray.
David stands at the bus stop with his hands in his pockets.
It is a little cold today, and the wind moves softly through the street.
David wears a dark jacket and carries a small bag for work.

He looks around.
 A woman drinks coffee from a paper cup.
 A student listens to music with headphones.

After a few more minutes, more people arrive at the bus stop.
 Everyone looks tired and silent.
David checks his phone one more time.
Then, finally, he sees the bus at the end of the street.
The bus stops in front of him, and the doors open.
David gets on the bus and finds a seat near the window.
Inside, it is warm and quiet.
He sits down, relaxes, and takes a deep breath.
questions: What time is it?
Why does David check his phone?
Is the bus on time?
How does David feel on the bus?
answers_q: It is 7:30 in the morning.
He is waiting for the bus.
No, the bus is late.
He feels better.
cloze: He finds a ___ on the bus.
speaking: “The bus is late again.”
example: “The bus is late again this morning.”

title: Buying a T-Shirt
content:
On Saturday afternoon, Maria goes to a clothes shop in the city center.
 She wants to buy a new T-shirt for summer.

The shop is bright and busy.
 Music plays softly inside, and many people walk between the shelves.

Maria looks at different T-shirts.
 There are red, white, green, and yellow ones.
But her favorite color is blue.
“Oh, this one is nice,” she says quietly.
She takes the blue T-shirt and goes to the fitting room.
A few minutes later, she looks at herself in the mirror.

The T-shirt fits very well.
 It is comfortable and looks good on her.
Then, she goes to the cashier with the T-shirt in her hands.
The cashier says hello and puts the T-shirt in a small bag.
Maria pays and thanks her politely.
Outside, the weather is sunny and warm.
She leaves the shop smiling and feels very happy with her choice.
questions: Why does Maria go to the shop?
What color does she like?
Does the T-shirt fit well?
How does Maria feel at the end?
answers_q: She wants a new T-shirt.
She likes the blue one.
Yes, it fits very well.
She feels happy.
speaking: “I like this one.”
example: “I like this blue T-shirt.”

title: At the Doctor
content:
John wakes up in the morning and does not feel well.
 His head hurts, and he feels very tired.
He drinks a little water, but he still feels bad.
Later, John decides to go to the doctor.

The doctor’s office is small and quiet.
 A few people are waiting in the room.
After some minutes, the doctor opens the door.
John stands up and goes inside.

The doctor smiles kindly and asks,
 “What is the problem?”

John sits down and answers,
 “I have a headache.”

The doctor checks him carefully.
 He listens to John and asks a few simple questions.
Then, the doctor gives John some medicine.
John thanks the doctor and goes home slowly.
At home, he takes the medicine and lies down in bed.
After some time, he feels better and relaxes.
questions: Why does John go to the doctor?
What problem does John have?
What does the doctor give him?
How does John feel later?
answers_q: He does not feel well.
He has a headache.
The doctor gives him medicine.
He feels better.
speaking: “I don’t feel well.”
example: “I don’t feel well. I have a headache.”

title: In the Office
content:
Lucy works in a large office in the city.
 Every morning, she arrives at 9 o’clock.

Today, the office is already busy when she arrives.
 Some people talk near the coffee machine, and others work quietly at their desks.
Lucy smiles and says hello to her colleagues.
Then, she sits at her desk and opens her computer.

She starts reading her emails carefully.
 Some messages are short, but others are important.
After that, Lucy writes a few messages and answers questions from her team.
At 12 o’clock, Lucy goes to lunch with two colleagues.
They sit together in a small lunch room and talk about their weekend.
Lucy laughs and enjoys the conversation.
In the afternoon, she returns to her desk and works again.
Outside, the sky slowly becomes darker.
At 5 o’clock, Lucy turns off her computer, takes her bag, and leaves the office.
She feels a little tired, but satisfied with her day.
questions: What time does Lucy arrive at work?
What does Lucy do on her computer?
Who does Lucy talk with at lunch?
What time does she go home?
answers_q: She arrives at 9 o’clock.
She reads emails and writes messages.
She talks with her colleagues.
She goes home at 5 o’clock.
speaking: “I have many emails today.”
example: “I have many emails today at work.”

title: A Phone Call
content:
In the evening, Mike sits on his sofa after work.
 The apartment is quiet, and he feels a little tired.
He looks at his phone and smiles.
“I want to talk to Anna,” he thinks.
After a few seconds, she answers the phone.
“Me too!” Anna says with a laugh.

They talk about their day together.
 Anna talks about work, and Mike talks about his long afternoon.
Outside, rain falls softly on the street.
Time passes quickly while they talk.
After ten minutes, Anna looks at the time.
“Yes, I need to sleep soon,” Mike answers.
They say goodbye and end the call.
Mike puts down his phone and smiles.
Now, he feels calm and happy.
questions: Who does Mike call?
How does Mike feel?
What do they talk about?
How long do they talk?
answers_q: He calls Anna.
He feels tired.
They talk about their day.
They talk for ten minutes.
speaking: “How are you today?”
example: “How are you today? I’m a little tired.”

title: At the Gym
content:
After work, Kevin goes to the gym near his apartment.
 He wants to exercise and feel better after a long day.
The gym is busy, and music plays loudly inside.
Kevin puts his bag in a locker and starts slowly.

First, he runs on a machine for ten minutes.
 At the beginning, he feels a little tired, but after some minutes, his body feels warm and active.
Then, Kevin goes to the weight area.
He lifts weights carefully and follows his exercise routine.
Some people exercise near him, and a coach talks to another man in the corner.
After a while, Kevin stops and drinks cold water.
He takes a deep breath and smiles.
Kevin continues his workout for one hour.
At the end, he is tired but happy.
He takes a shower in the gym and changes clothes.
Then, he goes home, sits on his sofa, and rests quietly for the evening.
questions: Why does Kevin go to the gym?
What does Kevin do first?
What does he drink?
How does Kevin feel after exercise?
answers_q: He wants to exercise.
He runs for ten minutes.
He drinks water.
He feels strong and happy.
cloze: After the gym, he takes a ___.
speaking: “I feel strong today.”
example: “I feel strong today after the gym.”

title: My Neighbor
content:
My neighbor is Mr. Brown.
 He lives in the apartment next to mine.
Mr. Brown is an older man, and he is always very friendly.
Every morning, when I leave for work, he smiles and says hello.
He has a small brown dog named Charlie.

Charlie is very playful and full of energy.
 When he sees people, he runs and moves his tail quickly.
I like the dog very much.
Sometimes, I meet Mr. Brown outside the building in the evening.
We stand near the entrance and talk together for a few minutes.
Usually, we talk about simple things like the weather or the neighborhood.
“It is very cold today,” Mr. Brown often says.
Mr. Brown is always calm, polite, and kind.
Good neighbors make life easier, and I am happy to know him.
questions: Who is Mr. Brown?
What is the dog’s name?
What do they talk about?
How is Mr. Brown?
answers_q: He is the neighbor.
The dog’s name is Charlie.
They talk about the weather and the neighborhood.
He is friendly and kind.
speaking: “Good morning! How are you?”
example: “Good morning! How are you today?”

title: At the Supermarket Again
content:
Today, I go to the supermarket after work.
 My refrigerator is almost empty, and I need some food for the week.
Outside, the weather is cool, and many people are in the street.
When I enter the supermarket, I take a basket near the door.

The supermarket is bright and busy.
 Some people push shopping carts, and others look at the shelves quietly.
I walk slowly through the different sections.

First, I buy milk and fresh bread.
 Then, I go to another section and take a box of eggs.
I also look at some fruit, but today I only buy a few things.
Near the checkout, there are many people waiting in line.
A child cries softly, and a woman talks on her phone.
I wait patiently with my basket.
I pay for my food, take my bag, and leave the supermarket.
I walk home slowly and feel good and relaxed.
questions: Why does the person go to the supermarket?
What does the person buy?
Are there many people in the supermarket?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person needs food.
The person buys milk, bread, and eggs.
Yes, there are many people.
The person feels good and relaxed.
cloze: I take a ___ at the supermarket.
I wait in ___ near the checkout.
speaking: “I only need a few things.”
example: “I only need a few things from the supermarket.”

title: A Visit to a Friend
content:
Today, I visit my friend in the afternoon.
 He lives near my house, so I walk there.
The weather is nice, and the streets are calm and quiet.
When I arrive, I ring the bell.
A few seconds later, my friend opens the door and smiles.
Inside, his home is warm and comfortable.
We sit in the living room and start talking about our week.
My friend makes tea for both of us.
The tea is hot, and it smells very good.
After some time, we turn on the TV and watch a funny program together.
Sometimes we laugh loudly at the same scenes.
Time passes quickly while we talk and relax.
Outside, the sun slowly goes down.
After two hours, I stand up and take my jacket.
Then, I walk home quietly and think about the nice afternoon.
It is a very good day.
questions: Where does the friend live?
What do they drink together?
What do they watch on TV?
How long does the visit last?
answers_q: He lives near the house.
They drink tea.
They watch a funny program.
The visit lasts two hours.
speaking: “It’s nice to see you.”
example: “It’s nice to see you again.”

title: My Night Routine
content:
At night, I finally go home after a long day.
 The streets are quieter now, and many lights shine in the buildings.
When I arrive home, I put my bag near the door and sit down for a moment.
I feel tired, but happy to be home.

First, I make dinner in the kitchen.
 Tonight, I eat rice and chicken with a glass of water.
After dinner, I clean the table and go to the living room.
I sit on the sofa and watch TV for some time.
The apartment feels calm and comfortable.
Later, I go to the bathroom and brush my teeth carefully.
Then, I change into comfortable clothes for the night.
At 10:30, I turn off the lights and go to bed.
Before sleeping, I read a few pages of a book.
The room is quiet, and I feel relaxed.
questions: How does the person feel at home?
What does the person do after dinner?
What time does the person go to bed?
What does the person read before sleeping?
answers_q: The person feels tired and relaxed.
The person watches TV.
The person goes to bed at 10:30.
The person reads a book.
speaking: “I’m ready for bed.”
example: “I’m ready for bed. Good night.”

title: The Morning Bus
content:
It is early in the morning, and the city is still quiet.
 The sky is gray, and the air feels cold.
Sophie walks to the bus stop with her bag on her shoulder.
She looks at the time on her phone.

Only a few people are waiting at the bus stop today.
 A man drinks coffee quietly, and a student listens to music.
Sophie waits with her hands in her coat pockets.
A few minutes later, the bus arrives at 7:40.
The doors open, and Sophie gets on the bus.
Inside, it is warm and comfortable.
She finds a seat near the window and sits down quietly.
As the bus moves through the city, Sophie looks outside.
She sees cars, bicycles, and people walking quickly to work.
Some shops are opening for the day.
After 20 minutes, the bus stops near her office.
Sophie stands up, takes her bag, and leaves the bus.
Now, she feels awake and ready for the day.
questions: Where does Sophie go in the morning?
How does she feel outside?
Where does Sophie sit on the bus?
How long is the bus ride?
answers_q: She goes to the bus stop.
She feels cold.
She sits near the window.
It is 20 minutes.
speaking: “It’s cold this morning.”
example: “It’s cold this morning. I need my coat.”

title: Buying Food
content:
Tom is hungry after a long morning.
 At lunchtime, he walks to a small shop near his office.
The shop is not very big, but it has many things to eat and drink.
Inside, Tom looks carefully at the sandwiches and snacks.
Some sandwiches have chicken, and others have cheese or vegetables.
After a few minutes, he chooses a chicken sandwich.
Then, he takes a bottle of cold water from the refrigerator.
Near the cashier, a few people wait quietly in line.
Tom waits patiently with his food in his hands.
When it is his turn, he pays and thanks the cashier.
Outside, the weather is sunny and pleasant.
Tom finds a small table near the shop and sits down.
Cars pass slowly in the street, and people walk nearby.
Tom eats his sandwich slowly and drinks some water.
He feels calm, relaxed, and ready for the rest of the day.
questions: Why does Tom go to the shop?
What sandwich does he choose?
What does Tom drink?
Where does Tom eat?
answers_q: He wants something to eat.
He chooses a chicken sandwich.
He drinks water.
He eats outside.
speaking: “I’m hungry. I need something to eat.”
example: “I’m hungry. I need something to eat now.”

title: A Morning Walk
content:
It is a quiet morning, and the streets are still calm.
 The air is cool, and the sky is light gray.
Lina puts on her shoes and goes outside for a walk.
She likes walking in the morning because everything feels peaceful.
As she walks slowly in the street, she hears birds and the sound of trees moving in the wind.
A few shops are opening, and some people walk to work with coffee in their hands.
Lina takes a deep breath and smiles softly.
After some minutes, she sees a man with a small dog.
The dog runs quickly from one side of the sidewalk to the other.
It looks very excited and playful.
Lina laughs quietly when the dog jumps happily near a tree.
The man smiles and says hello.
Lina continues walking slowly through the neighborhood.
Now, she feels happy and ready for the day.
questions: What is the weather like?
Why does Lina like morning walks?
What does the dog do?
How does Lina feel after the walk?
answers_q: The weather is cool.
The mornings feel peaceful.
The dog runs fast.
She feels happy and relaxed.
speaking: “I like walking in the morning.”
example: “I like walking in the morning when it is quiet.”

title: At the Office Again
content:
Paul arrives at work at 9 o’clock sharp.
 The office is already busy, and many people are at their desks.
He says hello to his colleagues and walks to his workspace.
Paul sits down, takes off his jacket, and turns on his computer.
While the computer starts, he drinks a cup of hot coffee.
Then, he begins reading his emails carefully.
Some emails are short questions, and others are important messages from clients.
Paul writes replies and checks his schedule for the day.
At noon, Paul goes to lunch with a colleague from his team.
They sit together in the office kitchen and talk about work and weekend plans.
After lunch, he returns to his desk and continues working.
Outside the window, the sky becomes darker, and the office slowly becomes quieter.
At 5 o’clock, Paul finishes his work for the day.
He turns off his computer, takes his bag, and leaves the office feeling satisfied.
questions: What time does Paul arrive at work?
What does Paul drink in the morning?
Who does Paul eat lunch with?
What time does Paul finish work?
answers_q: He arrives at 9 o’clock.
He drinks coffee.
He eats lunch with a colleague.
He finishes at 5 o’clock.
speaking: “I have a busy day today.”
example: “I have a busy day today at work.”

title: A Simple Lunch
content: It is lunchtime, and Emma feels very hungry after a busy morning at work.
She leaves the office and walks to a small café near the street corner.

The café is calm and not very crowded today.
 Soft music plays quietly inside.
Emma looks at the menu for a moment.
Finally, she orders a fresh salad and a bottle of water.
“Thank you,” she says politely to the waiter.
Then, she sits at a small table near the window.
Outside, people walk quickly through the city, and cars pass slowly in the street.
Emma eats her lunch slowly and enjoys the quiet moment.
The salad is fresh, and the cold water feels good.
For a few minutes, she forgets about work and relaxes.
After lunch, Emma checks the time on her phone.
“Oh, I need to go,” she thinks.
She pays for her meal, takes her bag, and leaves the café.
Then, she walks back to work feeling calm and ready for the afternoon.
questions: Why does Emma go to the café?
What does she order?
Where does Emma sit?
Where does she go after lunch?
answers_q: She is hungry.
She orders a salad and water.
She sits near the window.
She goes back to work.
cloze: After lunch, she goes back to ___.
speaking: “I’m hungry. Let’s eat.”
example: “I’m hungry. Let’s eat lunch.”

title: My Evening Walk
content: In the evening, after dinner, I decide to go outside for a walk.
The day is almost over, and the streets are quieter now.
I walk slowly in my street with my hands in my pockets.

I feel tired after the day, but happy to relax at home.
First, I make dinner in the kitchen.
Sometimes, I eat pasta or rice with vegetables and drink a glass of water.
After dinner, I clean the table and go to the living room.
Then, I watch TV for some time.
I like quiet programs or simple movies in the evening because they help me relax.
Later, I go to the bathroom and brush my teeth carefully.
I change into comfortable clothes and prepare for bed.
At 10:30, I turn off most of the lights and get into bed.
Before sleeping, I read a little from a book on my bedside table.
The room is calm and silent.
Soon, my eyes close slowly, and I fall asleep peacefully.
I usually sleep very well every night.
questions: How does the person feel after work?
What does the person do after dinner?
What time does the person go to bed?
What does the person read before sleeping?
answers_q: The person feels tired.
The person watches TV.
The person goes to bed at 10:30.
The person reads a book.
speaking: “I’m ready to sleep.”
example: “I’m ready to sleep. Good night.”

title: Asking for Directions
content: I am in the city today, but I do not know the area very well.
There are many streets, shops, and people around me.
I look at my phone, then at the street signs, but I still cannot find the station.
“I think I’m lost,” I say quietly.
For a moment, I feel a little stressed.
Then, I see a woman standing near a café.
She looks friendly, so I walk to her slowly.
The woman points down the street and explains slowly.
I listen carefully and repeat the directions quietly in my head.
Then, I continue walking through the city.
A few minutes later, I finally see the station in front of me.
Now, I feel relieved and happy.
questions: Why is the person stressed?
Who helps the person?
What directions does the woman give?
Does the person find the station?
answers_q: The person is lost.
A woman helps the person.
She says, “Go straight, then turn left.”
Yes, the person finds the station.
speaking: “Excuse me, where is the station?”
example: “Excuse me, where is the station, please?”

title: Buying a Ticket
content: I go to the station early in the morning because I need to take a train.
The station is busy, and many people walk quickly with bags and coffee cups.
I want to buy a ticket, so I walk to a large ticket machine near the entrance.
I look at the screen carefully, but I do not understand the instructions.
“There are too many buttons,” I think.
For a moment, I feel nervous and confused.
Then, I see a man standing nearby.

“Excuse me,” I say politely.
 “Can you help me?”
The man smiles kindly and comes closer.
He shows me what to press on the screen and explains everything slowly.
A few seconds later, the machine prints my ticket.
I take it and smile with relief.
Then, I walk to the platform and wait quietly for the train.
People stand around me, and an announcement comes from the speakers.
Now, I feel calm and much better.
questions: Where does the person go?
Why is the person confused?
Who helps the person?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person goes to the station.
The person does not understand the machine.
A man helps the person.
The person feels calm and better.

speaking: “Can you help me, please?”

title: At the Hotel
content: After a long trip, I finally arrive at the hotel in the evening.
Outside, the streets are quiet, and the lights of the city shine in the dark.
I walk into the hotel lobby with my bag in my hand.
The lobby is warm and calm, and soft music plays in the background.
I go to the front desk and smile politely.
The woman at the desk smiles kindly and checks her computer.
Then, she gives me a key and explains where my room is.
I thank her and take the elevator upstairs.
When I open the door, I see a small but very clean room.
There is a bed, a desk, and a small window with lights from the city outside.
I put my bag down near the bed and sit for a moment.
My body feels heavy after the trip.
I change clothes, turn off the light, and lie down in bed.
Soon, I close my eyes and fall asleep quickly.
questions: Where does the person arrive?
What does the person say at the desk?
How is the room?
How does the person feel?
answers_q: The person arrives at a hotel.
The person says, “I have a reservation.”
The room is small but clean.
The person feels tired.

example: “Hello, I have a reservation for tonight.”

title: A Problem in the Room
content: I am in my hotel room after a long trip.
The room is small, quiet, and clean.
I put my bag near the bed and want to turn on the light.
The room becomes darker because it is evening outside.
I look around for another light, but it does not work either.
For a moment, I feel tired and frustrated.
Then, I decide to go back downstairs to the front desk.
A man is standing there and working on the computer.

“Excuse me,” I say politely.
 “There is a problem in my room.”
The man listens carefully and nods.
“The light does not work,” I explain.

“I understand,” he says kindly.
 “I will help you.”
A few minutes later, he gives me another room and a new key.
This room is bright and comfortable.
Now, I feel calm and much better.
questions: What problem is in the room?
Where does the person go for help?
What does the man do?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The light does not work.
The person goes to the front desk.
He gives another room.
The person feels better.

title: At the Restaurant (Full Interaction)
content: In the evening, I go to a small restaurant near the city center because I feel hungry after work.
The restaurant is warm and busy, and soft music plays quietly inside.
A waiter comes to me with a smile and gives me a menu.

“Hello,” he says politely.
 “What do you want?”
I look at the menu for a moment and choose my meal.

The waiter writes it down and asks,
 “Do you want water?”
A few minutes later, he brings my food and a glass of water to the table.
The chicken smells very good, and the meal looks fresh and hot.
I start eating slowly while I look outside the window.
People walk through the street, and cars pass by quietly.
The food is delicious, and I enjoy every bite.
When I finish my meal, I smile at the waiter.
The waiter brings the bill, and I pay at the table.
Then, I thank him, leave the restaurant, and walk home feeling full and happy.
questions: Why does the person go to the restaurant?
What meal does the person order?
What does the waiter ask?
How is the meal?
answers_q: The person feels hungry.
The person orders chicken.
He asks, “Do you want water?”
The meal is very good.
speaking: “Can I have the bill, please?”
example: “Can I have the bill, please? Thank you.”

title: Saying Sorry
content: I walk in the street on a busy afternoon.
Many people are outside, and the sidewalks are full.
Some people talk on the phone, others carry shopping bags or drink coffee.
I look at a shop window for a moment while I continue walking.
Suddenly, I hit a man by accident.
The man looks at me for a second.
For a moment, I feel nervous and embarrassed.
But then, the man smiles kindly.
I relax a little and smile back at him.
The man adjusts his bag and continues walking down the street.
I watch him leave and take a deep breath.
At first, I feel a little bad because I do not like making mistakes.
But after a few seconds, I feel better again.
Sometimes, small accidents happen in busy places.
Then, we both continue our day and say goodbye politely.
questions: Where does the person walk?
What happens in the street?
What does the man say?
How does the person feel later?
answers_q: The person walks in the street.
The person hits a man by accident.
He says, “It’s okay.”
The person feels better.
speaking: “Oh, sorry!”
example: “Oh, sorry! Are you okay?”

title: Asking for Help
content: I am in a large shop today because I want to buy something for my apartment.
The shop has many different products, and there are long shelves everywhere.
I walk slowly and look around, but I cannot find what I need.
For a moment, I feel confused.
Then, I see a woman working in the shop.
She is organizing products near one of the shelves.

I walk to her politely and say,
 “Excuse me.”
The woman looks at me and smiles.
Then, she walks with me through the shop and shows me the product I am looking for.
“Oh, I understand now,” I say with relief.
The product is exactly where she says it is.
I smile and thank her politely.
Now, I feel much better and continue shopping calmly.
questions: Where is the person?
Why does the person need help?
Who helps the person?
How does the person feel at the end?

The person feels better.

title: At the Pharmacy
content: Today, I do not feel very well.
My head hurts, and I feel tired and weak.
After work, I decide to go to the pharmacy near my apartment.
The pharmacy is clean, quiet, and bright.
A woman stands behind the counter and smiles at me.
The woman listens carefully and asks a few simple questions.
Then, she takes a small box of medicine from a shelf behind her.
She explains slowly how to use it.
“Take one in the morning and one at night,” she says.
I listen carefully because I want to feel better quickly.
I thank her politely and pay for the medicine.
Outside, the weather is cool, and the street is calm.
I walk home slowly and drink some water when I arrive.
Then, I take the medicine and rest on the sofa.
A few hours later, my headache becomes smaller.
Now, I feel much better and more relaxed.
questions: Why does the person go to the pharmacy?
What problem does the person have?
What does the woman give?
How does the person feel later?
answers_q: The person feels sick.
The person has a headache.
The woman gives medicine.
The person feels better.
speaking: “I have a headache.”
example: “I have a headache and I feel tired.”

title: Making a Plan
content: Today, I talk to my friend on the phone after lunch.
We both have free time this afternoon, so we decide to make a plan together.
Then, we start talking about the time and the place.
At first, we are not sure where to go.
“It’s a little cold today,” I answer.
Finally, we choose a small café in the city center.
Then, we decide on the time.
“3 o’clock is good for me,” I say.
Later in the afternoon, I walk to the café.
My friend is already there waiting near the door.
We smile, say hello, and go inside together.
The café is warm and comfortable.
We order coffee, sit near the window, and talk for a long time.
Sometimes we laugh, and sometimes we simply relax quietly.
Outside, people walk through the busy streets.
For us, it is a simple but very good afternoon.
questions: Who does the person talk to?
What do they decide to do?
What time do they meet?
Where do they go?
answers_q: The person talks to a friend.
They make a plan to meet.
They meet at 3 o’clock.
They go to a café.
speaking: “Let’s meet at the café.”
example: “Let’s meet at the café at 3 o’clock.”

title: I Don’t Understand
content: I am in an English class this morning.
The classroom is quiet, and the teacher stands in front of the board.
She explains a lesson and writes new words on the board.
The other students listen carefully and take notes.
At first, I try to follow the lesson, but after some minutes, I feel confused.
The teacher speaks quickly, and I do not understand everything.

I look at my notebook and think,
 “What does this mean?”
For a moment, I feel nervous because I do not want to make a mistake.
But then, I take a deep breath and raise my hand slowly.
The teacher looks at me kindly.
The teacher smiles and explains the lesson again.
This time, she speaks more slowly and gives simple examples.
“Oh, okay!” I say with a smile.
I feel relieved, more confident, and happy that I asked for help.
questions: Where is the person?
Why does the person feel confused?
What does the teacher do?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person is in a class.
The person does not understand the lesson.
The teacher explains again slowly.
The person feels happy and relieved.
speaking: “I don’t understand. Can you explain again?”
example: “I don’t understand. Can you explain again, please?”

title: At the Bank
content: Today, I go to the bank because I need some money.
The bank is in the city center near a busy street.
When I arrive, many people are already inside.
Some people sit and wait quietly, and others speak with workers at the desks.
I take a number and wait in line patiently.
For a few minutes, I look around the bank and check my phone.
Then, a woman calls my number.
I walk to her desk and smile politely.
“Hello. How can I help you?” she asks kindly.
I explain that I need money from my account.
The woman listens carefully and works on her computer for a moment.
Then, she gives me the money and some papers.
I take the money and put it safely into my bag.
“Thank you very much,” I say politely.
“You’re welcome,” the woman answers with a smile.
Then, I leave the bank and walk back into the busy street feeling relieved.
questions: Why does the person go to the bank?
What does the person do before talking to the woman?
What does the woman give?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person needs money.
The person waits in line.
The woman gives money.
The person feels relieved.
speaking: “I need some money from my account.”
example: “I need some money from my account, please.”

title: Sending a Package
content: Today, I go to the post office because I need to send a package to my friend.
I carry a small box in my hands while I walk through the city.
When I arrive, the post office is busy.
Many people wait in line with letters, envelopes, and packages.
I stand quietly and wait for my turn.
While I wait, I look at the posters on the wall and check the address on my box again.
After a few minutes, a worker calls me to the desk.
I explain that I want to send the package.
The worker takes the box carefully and checks the address.
Then, she types information into her computer and tells me the price.
I pay for the service and receive a small receipt.
“Keep this,” she says with a smile.
I put the receipt safely in my bag and leave the post office feeling relieved.
Now, I know my package is on its way.
questions: Why does the person go to the post office?
What does the worker take?
What does the worker give the person?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person wants to send a package.
She takes the box.
She gives a receipt.
The person feels relieved and good.
speaking: “I would like to send this package.”
example: “I would like to send this package, please.”

title: Calling for Information
content: Today, I need a hotel room for next weekend.
I sit at my kitchen table with my phone and look for hotels on the internet.
After a few minutes, I find a small hotel in the city center.
The pictures look nice, so I decide to call.
I take a deep breath and dial the number.
After a few seconds, a man answers the phone.

“Hello,” I answer.
 “Do you have a room available?”
I feel relieved and continue the conversation.
I ask about the price and listen carefully to the information.
The man explains everything slowly and clearly.
The room sounds comfortable and not too expensive.
I write down the price and some details on a piece of paper.
“Thank you very much for your help,” I say.
Then, I finish the call and put down my phone.
Now, I feel calm and happy because I have the information I need.
questions: Why does the person call the hotel?
Who answers the phone?
What does the person ask about?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person wants a room.
A man answers the phone.
The person asks about the price.
The person feels calm and happy.
speaking: “Do you have a room available?”
example: “Hello, do you have a room available for tonight?”

title: A Lost Bag
content: I am at the station in the afternoon, waiting for my train.
The station is very busy, and many people walk quickly with suitcases and backpacks.
I sit down near the platform for a few minutes and check my phone.
Then, suddenly, I look beside me and stop.
I stand up quickly and look around.
“Oh no… where is my bag?” I think.
For a moment, I feel very worried.
I look near the seats and under the bench, but I still cannot find it.
Then, I see a worker near the information desk and walk to him quickly.

“Excuse me,” I say nervously.
 “I lost my bag.”
The worker listens carefully and asks me a few questions.

“What color is the bag?”
 “Where did you see it last?”
I answer slowly and try to remember everything.
Then, the worker helps me look around the station.
A few minutes later, we find the bag near another seat close to the platform.
“Thank you very much!” I say happily.
questions: Where is the person?
What is missing?
Who helps the person?
Do they find the bag?
answers_q: The person is at the station.
The bag is missing.
A worker helps the person.
Yes, they find the bag.
speaking: “I lost my bag.”
example: “Excuse me, I lost my bag.”

title: At the Taxi
content: It is evening, and I need to go home quickly after a long day.
The streets are busy, and many cars move slowly through the city.
I look around for a taxi near the corner of the street.
After a few moments, I see one coming toward me.
I raise my hand, and the taxi stops near the sidewalk.
I open the door, get inside, and sit comfortably in the back seat.
The taxi is warm, and soft music plays quietly on the radio.
The driver turns around and smiles politely.
“Where do you want to go?” he asks.
I say my address slowly and clearly.
Then, he starts driving through the city.
Outside the window, I see lights, shops, and people walking at night.
The ride is calm, and traffic is not too bad today.
We arrive at my building quickly.
I take out my wallet and pay the driver.
“Thank you,” I say before leaving the taxi.
Then, I close the door and walk home feeling relaxed.

Where does the person sit?
What does the person give to the driver?
How is the taxi ride?
answers_q: The person wants to go home quickly.
The person sits in the back seat.
The person gives the address and money.
The ride is calm.
speaking: “Can you take me to this address?”
example: “Can you take me to this address, please?”

title: Making an Appointment
content: Today, I need to see the doctor because I do not feel very well.
In the morning, I sit at the kitchen table with my phone and decide to call the doctor’s office.
I feel a little nervous because I do not like making phone calls.
After a few seconds, a woman answers the phone politely.

“Hello,” I answer slowly.
 “I want an appointment with the doctor, please.”
The woman asks me a few questions and checks the schedule on her computer.
“What day is good for you?” she asks.
I think for a moment and choose Monday because I am free that day.
“Monday is good for me,” I say.
The woman looks again at the schedule.
“Okay. The appointment is at 10 o’clock,” she explains.
I take a pen quickly and write the time in my notebook so I do not forget it.
“Thank you very much,” I say politely.
Then, I finish the call feeling organized and relieved.
questions: Why does the person call the doctor?
Who answers the phone?
What day does the person choose?
What time is the appointment?
answers_q: The person needs an appointment.
A woman answers the phone.
The person chooses Monday.
The appointment is at 10 o’clock.
cloze: I write the ___ in my notebook.
speaking: “I would like an appointment, please.”
example: “I would like an appointment for Monday, please.”

title: Asking the Time
content: I am in the street on a busy afternoon.
Many people walk quickly around me, and cars move slowly through the city.
I look at the sky and realize that I do not know the time.
I want to meet a friend later, so I need to know if I am late.
I search in my bag for my phone, but I cannot find it.
For a moment, I feel a little stressed.
Then, I see a man standing near a bus stop.

I walk to him politely and say,
 “Excuse me.”
The man looks at his watch for a second.
“Oh, okay. Thank you very much,” I say with a smile.
Now, I feel better because I know I still have time before meeting my friend.
Then, I continue walking through the city calmly.
questions: Why does the person ask for the time?
Where is the man standing?
What time is it?
How does the person feel after asking?
answers_q: The person wants to know if they are late.
The man is near a bus stop.
It is 3 o’clock.
The person feels better.
speaking: “Excuse me, what time is it?”
example: “Excuse me, what time is it, please?”

title: A Problem at Work
content: I am at work on a busy morning.
The office is quiet, and everyone is working at their desks.
I sit down, turn on my computer, and prepare to start my work.
But after a few seconds, I notice a problem.
My computer does not work correctly.
The screen stays black, and nothing happens.
I try again and press a few buttons.
Still, the computer does not work.
For a moment, I feel stressed because I have important work to do today.
Then, I decide to call a colleague for help.
A few minutes later, he comes to my desk.
“My computer does not work,” I explain.
He checks the cables and looks at the screen carefully.
After a short moment, he smiles.
Then, the computer suddenly starts working again.
I thank my colleague for his help.
Now, I feel relieved and continue working calmly.
questions: Where is the person?
What problem happens?
Who helps the person?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person is at work.
The computer does not work.
A colleague helps the person.
The person feels relieved.
speaking: “My computer is not working.”
example: “My computer is not working. Can you help me?”

title: Meeting New People
content: Tonight, I go to a small party at a friend’s apartment.
When I arrive, I hear music and people talking and laughing inside.
The apartment is warm and full of people.
Some people stand near the window, and others sit on the sofa with drinks in their hands.
At first, I feel a little nervous because I do not know many people there.
Then, I see a man standing near the table with snacks and drinks.

He smiles at me and says,
 “Hello.”

“Hello,” I answer politely.
 “My name is John.”
The man tells me his name, and we start talking together.
At first, the conversation is simple.
We talk about the music, the party, and the city.
Slowly, I start to feel more comfortable.
We both smile and laugh a little during the conversation.
Then, we take drinks and continue talking for a few more minutes.
The atmosphere feels friendly and relaxed.
I am happy because meeting new people is sometimes difficult for me.
But tonight feels easy and pleasant.
It is really nice to meet him.
questions: Where does the person go?
How does the person feel at first?
What do the two people do together?
How is the meeting?
answers_q: The person goes to a party.
The person feels nervous at first.
They talk and drink something together.
The meeting is nice.
speaking: “Nice to meet you.”
example: “Nice to meet you. My name is John.”

title: Calling for Help
content: I am at home one evening when I suddenly start to feel very sick.
My head hurts, and I feel weak and dizzy.
At first, I sit on the sofa and try to rest for a few minutes.
But after some time, I realize the problem is serious.
I feel scared and decide to call for help.
My hands shake a little while I hold my phone.
After a few seconds, a man answers calmly.
“Hello, how can I help you?” he asks.
I explain my problem slowly because I do not feel well.
The man listens carefully and speaks in a calm voice.

“Okay,” he says.
 “Help is coming. Please stay calm.”
His words make me feel a little safer.
I sit quietly and wait in the living room.
Outside, I hear cars passing in the street.
A few minutes later, someone knocks on the door.
The people speak kindly and help me immediately.
Now, I feel safer, calmer, and less afraid.
questions: Where is the person?
How does the person feel?
What does the man say on the phone?
How does the person feel at the end?
answers_q: The person is at home.
The person feels very sick.
He says help is coming.
The person feels safe.
speaking: “I need help.”
example: “I need help. I feel very sick.”

title: A Busy Morning
content: Tom usually woke up at 7:00 every morning. He liked having enough time before work. He normally took a shower, made coffee, and ate toast while listening to music in the kitchen.
When Tom opened his eyes, the room was already bright. He looked at the clock next to his bed and suddenly sat up.

He felt stressed immediately. His bus normally came at 8:25, and he was still in bed. He quickly got out of bed and almost dropped his phone on the floor. His room was messy, and he could not find a clean shirt.
After a few seconds, he found one on a chair near the window. Then he ran to the bathroom and took a very fast shower. He looked at himself in the mirror and laughed a little.
Usually, Tom loved breakfast, especially eggs and coffee. But that morning, he did not have time to prepare anything. He only drank a little water before leaving the apartment.
Outside, the weather was cold and windy. Tom ran quickly to the bus stop with his bag on one shoulder. He was breathing hard when he arrived.
Luckily, the bus was still there.
The driver looked at him and smiled.
He got on the bus and sat down near the back. His heart was still beating fast, but little by little, he started to relax. He closed his eyes for a moment and listened to the sound of the bus.
When he arrived at work, he walked quickly to his office. His boss looked at him with surprise.
Tom fixed his shirt and smiled.
“Yes, I’m just a little tired today.”
A few hours later, after some coffee and work, Tom finally felt better. By the afternoon, his stressful morning already felt far away.
questions: Why did Tom feel stressed?
What did Tom usually do before work?
Did Tom eat breakfast that morning?
What did the bus driver ask Tom?
How did Tom feel later at work?
answers_q: He felt stressed because he woke up late.
He usually took a shower, made coffee, and ate toast.
No, he did not.
The driver asked, “Busy morning?”
He felt better later.
cloze: Tom woke up very ______ yesterday morning.
He did not have time for ______.
After a few hours, Tom felt ______.
speaking: You arrive late at work and talk to your manager.
example: “Sorry, I’m late. I woke up late this morning and missed my usual routine.”
grammar: He ran to the bus stop.
Used to talk about finished actions in the past.

title: A Trip to Another City
content: Last weekend, Anna visited another city for the first time. She needed a short break from her daily routine and wanted to discover a new place. For several days, she had felt tired because of work, so she decided to travel alone for the weekend.

On Saturday morning, she arrived at the train station very early. The station was already busy. Some people were drinking coffee, others were looking at the departure screens, and many travelers were carrying big bags.

Anna found her train and sat near the window. The journey was long, but comfortable. She put on her headphones, listened to music, and looked outside. The weather was cloudy at first, but after an hour, the sun appeared. She watched fields, small villages, and rivers pass by.
At one point, an old woman sitting next to her smiled and asked, “Are you visiting family?”
Anna smiled back. “No, I just wanted to visit a new city for the weekend.”

After several hours, the train finally arrived. Anna felt excited when she stepped onto the platform. The city was bigger than she expected. There were tall buildings, busy streets, and many people everywhere.
She walked to her hotel with her small suitcase. The room was small but clean and comfortable. There was a little desk, a bed near the window, and soft yellow lights on the walls.
After a short rest, Anna went outside again. In the afternoon, she walked around the city center. She saw many shops, restaurants, and street musicians. The city felt alive and energetic.
She stopped several times to take pictures of old buildings and colorful streets. Later, she entered a small souvenir shop and bought a few postcards and a key ring for her brother.
By the evening, she started to feel hungry. She chose a quiet restaurant near the river and ordered pasta and tea. While she was eating, she watched people walk outside under the evening lights.
At the end of the day, Anna felt happy and relaxed. She was tired, but it was a good kind of tiredness. Before going to sleep, she looked at her pictures again and smiled.
“I really needed this trip,” she thought.
questions: Why did Anna decide to travel?
What did Anna do during the train journey?
How was the hotel room?
What did Anna buy in the souvenir shop?
How did Anna feel at the end of the day?
answers_q: She wanted a break from her routine.
She listened to music and looked outside.
It was small but comfortable.
She bought postcards and a key ring.
She felt happy and relaxed.
cloze: She took many ______ in the city.
In the evening, she ate at a ______.
speaking: You are talking to a friend about a recent trip.
example: “I visited another city last weekend. I traveled by train and took many pictures. The trip was very relaxing.”
grammar: Used to describe past actions and travel experiences.

title: A Problem at the Hotel
content: Last night, I stayed in a hotel in the city center. I arrived there around 8:00 in the evening after a long train journey. I was very tired and only wanted to rest.

The receptionist welcomed me with a smile and gave me my room key. The hotel looked modern and clean. There were soft lights in the lobby and quiet music in the background. My room was on the fourth floor. It was not very big, but it looked comfortable. There was a large bed, a small desk, and a television near the window.
I took a shower, changed my clothes, and lay down on the bed. I finally started to relax.
But later that night, I suddenly heard a loud noise in the hallway.

At first, I thought it would stop after a few minutes. But the noise continued. I heard people talking loudly, doors opening and closing, and someone laughing near my room. I looked at the clock. It was almost midnight.
I tried to sleep again, but it was impossible.

After another twenty minutes, I decided to go to the reception desk. I put on my shoes and went downstairs. The lobby was much quieter than before. The receptionist was working on his computer when I arrived.
“Excuse me,” I said politely. “There is a lot of noise near my room. I can’t sleep.”
The receptionist listened carefully and looked concerned.
“I am very sorry,” he said. “Some guests arrived late tonight.”
He checked something on the computer for a few seconds.
“Fortunately, we still have another room available,” he explained. “It is on the top floor and much quieter.”
A few minutes later, he gave me a new key card. The second room was smaller, but it was very quiet. I could not hear anything outside.
I finally got into bed again and closed my eyes. This time, the room was peaceful, and I fell asleep very quickly.
The next morning, I woke up feeling rested and much happier.
questions: Why was the person tired at the beginning of the story?
What noise did the person hear at night?
Why did the person go to the reception?
What did the receptionist do?
How did the person feel the next morning?
answers_q: Because of a long train journey.
Loud people, doors, and laughter.
Because they could not sleep.
He gave another room.
The person felt rested and happier.
cloze: The hotel room was on the ______ floor.
speaking: You are speaking to a hotel receptionist about a problem in your room.
example: “Excuse me, I can’t sleep because there is too much noise near my room.”
grammar: Used to describe past events and simple polite communication.

title: Meeting New Colleagues
content: Last week, I started a new job at a small marketing company. Before my first day, I felt excited, but also a little nervous. It had been a long time since I worked in a new place, and I did not know anyone there.
On Monday morning, I woke up earlier than usual. I wanted everything to go well. I chose my clothes carefully, prepared my bag, and left home with enough time to arrive early.
When I arrived at the office building, I took a deep breath before entering.
At the reception desk, a woman smiled at me.
“Good morning! You must be the new employee,” she said kindly.

A few minutes later, my manager came to meet me. He showed me around the office and introduced me to my new colleagues. At first, I was worried because everyone already knew each other. But very quickly, I started to feel more relaxed.
My colleagues were friendly and welcoming. One person offered me coffee, another helped me connect my computer, and everyone smiled when they spoke to me.
“This is your desk,” my manager explained.
My desk was near a large window with a nice view of the street. There was also a small plant on the table.
“I think someone left this for you,” one colleague joked.
Everybody laughed softly, and I smiled too.
During the morning, we talked about our work and the different projects in the company. Some things were new for me, so I listened carefully and asked a few questions.
At lunchtime, my colleagues invited me to eat with them in a nearby café. I was happy because I did not want to eat alone on my first day.

Over lunch, the conversation became more relaxed. We talked about music, movies, travel, and hobbies. One colleague loved photography, another enjoyed cooking, and someone else played basketball every weekend.
“What about you?” a colleague asked me.
“I like reading and walking in the city,” I answered.
After a few days, I started to feel much more comfortable at work. I learned people’s names, understood my tasks better, and became more confident.
Now, when I arrive at the office in the morning, I no longer feel nervous.
I actually enjoy my job and look forward to seeing my colleagues every day.
questions: Why was the person nervous at first?
How were the colleagues?
Where was the desk located?
What did the colleagues do during lunch?
How did the person feel after a few days?
answers_q: Because it was a new job.
They were friendly and welcoming.
Near a large window.
They talked about hobbies and interests.
The person felt more comfortable.
cloze: The colleagues showed the new employee a ______.

speaking: You are introducing yourself to new colleagues at work.
example: “Hi, my name is Sarah. I’m new here. I enjoy reading, music, and traveling.”
grammar: Used to describe past experiences and social interactions.

title: A Visit to the Doctor
content: Yesterday morning, I woke up feeling terrible. My head hurt, my body felt weak, and I was very tired. At first, I thought maybe I only needed more sleep, so I stayed in bed for another hour.
But when I finally got up, I still felt sick.
I went to the kitchen to make tea, but even the smell of food made me uncomfortable. I touched my forehead and realized I probably had a fever.
I tried to answer a few work emails on my phone, but it was difficult to concentrate because of my headache. After a while, I decided it was better to see a doctor.
Around 10:00, I left home and walked slowly to a nearby medical clinic. The weather outside was cold, and I felt tired after only a few minutes.

When I arrived, the waiting room was already full of people. Some patients were reading magazines, others were looking at their phones quietly. I checked in at the reception desk and sat down near the window.
I waited for about twenty minutes.
Finally, the doctor opened the door and called my name.
“Good morning,” he said kindly. “Please come in.”
Inside the office, the doctor asked me several questions.
“Yes, and I also have a headache.”
The doctor listened carefully while I explained how I felt. Then he checked my temperature and listened to my breathing.
After a few minutes, he smiled gently.
“It’s probably a virus,” he explained. “You need rest, water, and medicine.”
He wrote a prescription and told me to stay home for a couple of days.

After leaving the clinic, I stopped at a pharmacy to get the medicine. Then I went home, changed into comfortable clothes, and stayed in bed for the rest of the day. I watched television for a while and drank a lot of tea.
This morning, I woke up feeling much better. My headache was gone, and I finally had more energy again.
questions: What symptoms did the person have?
Why did the person decide to see a doctor?
How long did the person wait at the clinic?
What did the doctor recommend?
How did the person feel the next day?
answers_q: A headache, fever, and tiredness.
Because the person still felt sick.
About twenty minutes.
Rest, water, and medicine.
Much better.

The person stayed in ______ all day.
speaking: You are explaining your symptoms to a doctor.
example: “I have a headache and a fever. I also feel very tired today.”
grammar: Used to describe health problems and past actions.

title: A Missed Bus
content: Yesterday morning, I woke up later than usual. My alarm did ring, but I was very tired and stayed in bed for a few extra minutes. Unfortunately, those few minutes became much longer.
When I finally looked at the time, I immediately jumped out of bed.
“Oh no, I’m late!” I said loudly.

Everything felt rushed after that. I got dressed quickly, brushed my teeth, and put some papers into my bag. Usually, I drank coffee before leaving home, but yesterday I did not have time for breakfast or even a drink.
Before leaving, I checked the time again and realized I only had a few minutes to catch my bus.

Outside, the streets were already busy. Cars moved slowly through traffic, and many people were walking quickly to work or school. I walked fast at first, then started running when I saw the time on my phone.
“Maybe I can still catch it,” I thought.
When I finally arrived at the bus stop, I saw the bus driving away at the end of the street.
I stopped and looked at it in frustration.
For a few seconds, I felt very annoyed. I was already tired, and now I was going to arrive late at work too.
I sat down at the bus stop and checked the schedule on my phone. The next bus was coming in twenty minutes.
At first, waiting felt difficult. The morning air was cold, and I kept thinking about work. But after a few minutes, I decided to relax a little.

I took out my headphones and started listening to music. Slowly, I began to feel calmer. Around me, people came and went. A woman was reading a book, a student was drinking coffee, and an older man was feeding birds near the sidewalk.
After some time, I heard the sound of another bus approaching.
Finally, the next bus arrived exactly on time.
I got on, found a seat near the window, and took a deep breath. The situation was not perfect, but at least I was finally on my way to work.
When I arrived at the office, my colleague smiled and asked, “Busy morning?”
questions: Why did the person wake up late?
What happened when the person arrived at the bus stop?
How long was the wait for the next bus?
What did the person do while waiting?
What did the colleague ask at work?
answers_q: Because the person stayed in bed too long.
The bus had already left.
Twenty minutes.
The person listened to music.
“Busy morning?”

speaking: You are explaining to a colleague why you arrived late.
example: “Sorry, I missed my bus this morning, so I had to wait for the next one.”
grammar: I waited for the next bus.
Used to talk about transportation problems and past events.

title: A Dinner with Friends
content: Last night, I had dinner with my friends after a long week of work. We had not seen each other for several weeks, so everyone was excited to meet again.

We chose a restaurant in the city center that we all liked. It was a small Italian restaurant near a busy street with many cafés and shops. When I arrived, I could already see people talking and laughing inside through the windows.
My friends were already there waiting for me.
“Finally!” one of them joked when I entered.
“Sorry, the traffic was terrible,” I replied while laughing.

The restaurant was busy, but the atmosphere felt warm and friendly. Soft music was playing in the background, and the smell of pizza and fresh bread filled the room. A waiter showed us to our table near the back of the restaurant.
After sitting down, we looked at the menu together.
“I think I’m going to order pizza,” my friend Lucas said.
“That sounds good,” I replied. “But I really want pasta tonight.”

We also ordered drinks and some bread for the table. While waiting for the food, we started talking about our week. One friend talked about a stressful meeting at work, another explained her travel plans for the summer, and someone else shared funny stories about his little brother.
At one point, Lucas tried to tell a story, but he forgot an important part in the middle.
“Wait… no, that’s not what happened!” he said suddenly.
The evening became more relaxed and fun after that. We laughed a lot, shared stories, and forgot about work and daily stress for a few hours.
When the food finally arrived, it looked delicious. My pasta had a creamy sauce with chicken and mushrooms, and Lucas’s pizza smelled amazing.
“This is really good,” I said after my first bite.
“I told you this place was great,” Lucas replied proudly.
After dinner, we stayed at the table for a little longer because nobody wanted the evening to end too quickly. Later, we decided to walk together through the city streets before going home.
The night air was cool, and the city lights looked beautiful. People were still outside enjoying the evening, and the atmosphere felt calm and happy.
Before leaving, one friend smiled and said, “We should do this more often.”
It really was a great evening.
questions: Why were the friends happy to meet?
What kind of restaurant did they choose?
What food did the narrator order?
Why did everyone laugh during dinner?
What did the friends do after dinner?
answers_q: Because they had not seen each other for weeks.
An Italian restaurant.
Pasta.
Because Lucas forgot part of his story.
They walked together in the city.
cloze: The restaurant was busy but very ______.

speaking: You are talking to a friend about a recent dinner outing.
example: “Last night, I had dinner with friends at an Italian restaurant. The food was excellent, and we laughed a lot.”
grammar: Used to describe social activities and meals in the past

title: A Day at Work
content: Yesterday was a very busy day at work. When my alarm rang at 6:30 in the morning, I already knew the day would probably be stressful. The evening before, I had received several messages from my manager about an important project.
I arrived at work earlier than usual because I wanted enough time to organize everything before the office became busy.

When I entered the building, only a few people were there. The office was quiet, and the lights were still dim in some areas. I put my bag next to my desk, turned on my computer, and prepared a cup of coffee.
A few seconds later, my email inbox opened.
I looked at the screen and sighed softly.
There were messages from clients, coworkers, and my manager. Some emails were simple, but others needed detailed answers. I started reading them carefully and answered them one by one.
After almost an hour, I finally finished the most urgent emails.
At 10 o’clock, I had a meeting with my team in a conference room on the second floor. Everyone looked a little tired, but people were focused and ready to work.
During the meeting, we discussed an important project that needed to be finished before the end of the month. My manager explained the situation and asked for updates from each person.
When it was my turn, I presented my progress and shared a few ideas.
“That sounds good,” my manager said. “Let’s continue in that direction.”
Hearing that made me feel more confident.

The rest of the morning passed quickly. I spent time preparing documents, checking information, and answering more messages. Around lunchtime, I ate a sandwich at my desk because I did not have much free time.
In the afternoon, things became calmer. I focused on my remaining tasks and slowly completed everything on my list.
At around 4:30, I looked at my computer screen and smiled.
Finally, all my work was finished.
I felt tired after such a busy day, but I was also satisfied because everything had gone well.
At 5 o’clock, I packed my bag and left the office. Outside, the weather was pleasant and cool. On the way home, I listened to music and enjoyed the quiet moment after a long day.
In the evening, I stayed home, ate dinner, and relaxed on the sofa while watching television.
It was a busy day, but a productive one too.
questions: Why did the person arrive at work early?
What did the person do first at work?
What was the meeting about?
How did the person feel after the meeting?
What did the person do in the evening?
answers_q: To organize everything before work became busy.
The person answered emails.
An important project.
More confident.
The person relaxed at home.
cloze: The person had many ______ to read.
speaking: You are describing your workday to a friend or colleague.
example: “Yesterday was very busy at work. I had many emails, a meeting, and several tasks to finish.”
grammar: Used to describe professional activities and completed actions in the past.

title: A Visit to the Museum
content: Last Saturday, I visited a museum with my sister. We had talked about going there for several weeks, but we were both busy with work and daily life. Finally, we decided that Saturday was the perfect day for it.

The weather was cool but sunny when we left home in the morning. We took the metro together and talked during the journey. My sister really likes history and art, so she was especially excited about the visit.
“I heard this museum is amazing,” she said while looking at the map on her phone.
“I hope it’s not too crowded,” I replied.

When we arrived, we immediately saw a long building with large glass windows and stone walls. Many tourists and families were standing outside near the entrance. The museum looked much bigger than I expected.
Inside, the atmosphere was calm and quiet. People walked slowly from room to room while looking carefully at the different exhibitions.
There were many interesting things to see.
In the first section, we saw old paintings from different countries. Some paintings showed people from hundreds of years ago, while others showed nature, cities, or historical events.
One painting was very colorful and immediately caught my attention.
“I really like this one,” I told my sister.
“Me too,” she replied. “The colors are beautiful.”
Later, we visited another room with old objects from ancient times. We saw coins, clothes, tools, and even old furniture. Some objects were very small, but others were surprisingly large.

We also spent time reading the information written on the walls. Some explanations were simple, but others were more detailed. My sister liked reading everything carefully, while I preferred looking at the objects first.
At one point, we sat down on a bench because we were getting tired from walking.
“We’ve already been here for almost two hours,” my sister said with surprise.
Before leaving, we visited the museum shop and looked at postcards and books for a few minutes. Then we decided to go to a nearby café.

After the museum visit, sitting down with a hot coffee felt very relaxing. We talked about our favorite parts of the museum and laughed about some strange modern art pieces we did not fully understand.
By the end of the afternoon, we both felt happy and satisfied.
It was a simple day, but a very nice one.
questions: Who did the person visit the museum with?
What did they see in the museum?
Which object caught the narrator’s attention?
How long did they stay in the museum?
What did they do after the visit?
answers_q: With their sister.
Paintings and old objects.
A colorful painting.
About two hours.
They had coffee in a café.

example: “Last weekend, I visited a museum with my sister. We saw many paintings and learned interesting things.”
grammar: Used to describe cultural activities and past experiences

title: A Small Accident
content: Yesterday afternoon, I had a small accident while walking in the city center. It was not serious, but it was still a little embarrassing.

I had just finished work and decided to walk home because the weather was pleasant. The streets were busy with people shopping, talking, and sitting outside cafés. I was feeling relaxed and thinking about my evening plans.
At one point, I took out my phone to read a message from a friend.
“Do you still want to meet tomorrow?” the message said.
While I was reading and walking at the same time, I did not notice a small step in front of me.
Suddenly, my foot hit the edge of the sidewalk, and I lost my balance.
Before I understood what was happening, I fell down onto the street.
For a second, I stayed on the ground in surprise. My bag fell next to me, and a few people turned around to look at me.
A man who was walking nearby quickly came to help me.
“Are you okay?” he asked with concern.
“Yes, I think so,” I replied, still a little shocked.
He helped me stand up slowly and gave me my bag. I checked my hands and knees carefully. Luckily, I only had a small pain in my knee and a little dirt on my clothes.
“That step is difficult to see sometimes,” the man explained kindly.
A woman nearby smiled at me and asked if I needed water, but I told her I was fine. Even though I was not badly hurt, I still felt a little embarrassed because several people had seen me fall.
After taking a deep breath, I cleaned my jacket and fixed my bag.
“Thank you very much for your help,” I said to the man.
“No problem,” he replied with a smile. “Take care.”
Then I continued walking home, but this time I paid much more attention to the street.
Later that evening, I told the story to my friend on the phone. We both laughed about it.
“At least it wasn’t serious,” she said.
She was right. It was only a small accident, but I will definitely watch where I walk from now on.
questions: Where did the accident happen?
Why did the person fall?
Who helped the person?
Was the person seriously hurt?
How did the person feel after the accident?
answers_q: In the city street.
Because the person did not see a step.
A man nearby helped.
No, the injuries were small.
The person felt embarrassed.
