grammar: Used to describe accidents and past events.

title: A Shopping Day
content: Yesterday, I went shopping because I needed some new clothes for work. Most of my shirts were getting old, and one of my pairs of shoes was uncomfortable. I decided it was finally time to buy a few new things.
In the morning, I took the bus to a large shopping center on the other side of the city. The weather was sunny, and many people were already outside when I arrived.
The shopping center was very busy. Music was playing softly in the background, and people were walking from store to store with bags in their hands.
First, I went to a big clothing store. There were many options everywhere: shirts, jackets, jeans, shoes, and accessories in different colors and styles.
For a moment, I did not know where to start.
“Can I help you?” a shop assistant asked kindly.
“Yes,” I replied. “I’m looking for shirts for work.”
She showed me a section near the back of the store. I started looking through the clothes slowly. Some shirts were too bright, others were too large, and a few were too expensive.
After a while, I found several shirts that I liked and went to the fitting room to try them on.
The first shirt was black, but it felt too tight. The second one was white, but I did not really like the style. Then I tried on a blue shirt.
As soon as I looked in the mirror, I smiled.
“This one looks much better,” I thought.
The shirt was comfortable and simple, exactly what I wanted. I decided to buy it immediately.

After that, I visited the shoe section because I also needed new shoes. I tried on two different pairs. One pair looked good but felt uncomfortable after a few minutes. The second pair was lighter and softer.
“How do they feel?” the shop assistant asked.
So I bought those shoes too.
When I finished shopping, I went to the cashier with my items. There was a long line, so I had to wait for several minutes. Finally, it was my turn to pay.
The cashier smiled and asked, “Did you find everything you needed today?”
After leaving the store, I stopped for a coffee before going home. I felt tired from walking around all day, but I was also very satisfied with my purchases.
It was a good shopping day.

What color shirt did the person choose?
Why did the person buy the second pair of shoes?

answers_q: Because the person needed new clothes.

Happy and satisfied.

example: “Yesterday, I bought a blue shirt and comfortable shoes for work.”
grammar: Used to describe shopping activities and past actions.

title: A Phone Problem
content: Yesterday morning, my phone suddenly stopped working. Everything was normal at first. I woke up, checked my messages, and listened to music while preparing breakfast. Then, a few minutes later, the screen suddenly became black.
At first, I thought the battery was empty.
I connected the phone to the charger and waited for a few minutes, but nothing happened. The screen stayed black, and the phone did not make any sound.
I started to feel worried because I use my phone for many things every day. I use it for work emails, messages, maps, and online banking. Without it, I felt completely disconnected.
I tried to restart the phone several times. I pressed the buttons again and again, but it still did not work.

For a moment, I searched online on my laptop for possible solutions. Some websites said to wait a few minutes, while others suggested pressing different buttons together. I tried everything carefully, but the phone still would not turn on.
Finally, I decided to go to a phone repair shop near my apartment.
The shop was small but modern, with many phones and accessories displayed on the walls. A young worker behind the counter smiled when I entered.
“Hello, how can I help you?” he asked.
“My phone stopped working this morning,” I explained. “I tried restarting it, but nothing happens.”
“Let me take a look,” he replied.
He checked the phone carefully for several minutes. First, he connected it to another charger. Then he opened a small program on his computer and tested the battery.
After a short moment, he nodded.
“The problem is not serious,” he explained. “The system crashed, but I think I can fix it.”
Hearing that immediately made me feel calmer.
While he worked on the phone, I sat in a chair near the window and waited. Around fifteen minutes later, he smiled and handed the phone back to me.
I pressed the button, and the screen finally turned on again.
“Oh, thank you so much!” I said with relief.
Before leaving the shop, I checked my messages quickly. I had several missed notifications from friends and work.
On the way home, I realized how important my phone had become in daily life. I was very happy that the problem was fixed so quickly.

What did the person try before going to the shop?

grammar: Used to describe technology problems and past actions.

title: A Late Arrival
content: Yesterday morning, I arrived late to an important meeting at work. Usually, I am very careful about time, especially for meetings with my team, so the situation made me feel stressed and uncomfortable.

The meeting was supposed to start at 9:00, and I left home early enough to arrive before everyone else. At first, everything was normal. I took the bus and listened to music while checking a few notes on my phone.
But after several minutes, traffic suddenly became very heavy.
Cars stopped moving, and buses were moving very slowly through the streets. I looked outside the window and saw long lines of vehicles everywhere.
A few minutes later, the bus driver made an announcement.
“There has been a small accident ahead,” he explained. “Traffic may be slow for a while.”
Immediately, I checked the time on my phone and started feeling nervous.
I still hoped to arrive on time, but traffic became worse and worse. At one point, I even thought about leaving the bus and walking, but the office was still too far away.
I sent a quick message to one of my colleagues.
“I may be a little late. Traffic is terrible today.”
“No problem,” she replied. “We’ll wait for you if possible.”
That message made me feel slightly better, but I still hated being late.
When I finally arrived at the office building, I walked quickly to the meeting room. I could already hear people talking inside.
I opened the door quietly and looked at everyone.
“Sorry for being late,” I said immediately. “There was a lot of traffic this morning.”
My colleagues understood the situation, and nobody seemed angry. I sat down quickly, opened my notebook, and started listening carefully to the discussion.
The meeting was about a new project for an important client. I focused on the conversation and took notes during the presentation so I would not miss important information.
Little by little, I started feeling calmer.
After the meeting ended, one colleague smiled at me and said, “Don’t worry, traffic was bad everywhere today.”
“That makes me feel better,” I answered.
Even though the morning started badly, the rest of the day went much more smoothly.

What caused the traffic?
Who did the person message?
What did the person do during the meeting?
How did the person feel after the meeting?
answers_q: Because of heavy traffic.
A small accident.

Better and calmer.

example: “Sorry I’m late. There was a lot of traffic this morning.”
grammar: Used to describe workplace situations and past events.

title: A Weekend at Home
content: Last weekend, I decided to stay at home and rest. The previous week had been very busy, and by Friday evening I felt completely tired. I did not want to travel, go shopping, or make complicated plans. I only wanted a quiet weekend.
On Saturday morning, I woke up much later than usual. Normally, I set an alarm during the week, but this time I turned my phone off and slept peacefully for several extra hours.
When I finally got out of bed, the apartment was silent and calm. Outside, the weather was rainy and cold, which made staying inside feel even more comfortable.
“This is the perfect weekend to relax,” I thought.

I made myself a cup of tea and prepared a simple breakfast. Then I sat on the sofa with a blanket and started watching movies. I chose an old comedy first, and later I watched a detective film that was much more serious.

During the afternoon, I also spent some time reading a book that I had started a few weeks earlier. Usually, I do not have enough time to read during busy workdays, so it felt nice to read slowly without checking the time.
The book was very interesting. It was about a man traveling alone through different countries and meeting new people. Some parts were funny, and others were emotional.
At one point, I looked outside the window and realized it was already getting dark.
“I’ve done almost nothing today,” I said to myself while smiling.
But honestly, that was exactly what I needed.

Throughout the weekend, I cooked simple meals at home. On Saturday evening, I made pasta with vegetables, and on Sunday I prepared soup and rice. Nothing was complicated, but everything felt warm and comforting.
I also drank a lot of tea while listening to quiet music in the background. The peaceful atmosphere helped me forget about stress and work for a while.
On Sunday evening, I cleaned my apartment a little and prepared my clothes for Monday morning. Then I sat down again with another cup of tea and reflected on the weekend.
I felt relaxed, calm, and full of energy again.
The weekend was simple, but sometimes simple moments are the best ones.
By the end of Sunday evening, I was finally ready for the new week.
questions: Why did the person stay at home?
What was the weather like during the weekend?
What did the person do on the sofa?
What kind of meals did the person cook?
How did the person feel on Sunday evening?
answers_q: Because the person needed rest.
Rainy and cold.
Watched movies and relaxed.
Simple meals like pasta and soup.
Relaxed and ready for the week.
cloze: The person slept a ______ during the weekend.

speaking: You are describing a relaxing weekend at home.
example: “Last weekend, I stayed home, watched movies, read a book, and relaxed a lot.”
grammar: Used to describe relaxing activities and past routines

title: A Train Journey
content: Yesterday, I took a train to visit a friend in another city. I had not traveled by train for a long time, so I was excited before the trip. My train was scheduled to leave early in the morning, so I woke up before sunrise and quickly prepared my bag.

Outside, the streets were still quiet when I left home. The air was cold, and only a few people were walking near the station. When I arrived at the train station, I saw many travelers with suitcases, coffee cups, and backpacks.
I checked the departure board carefully and found my platform.

A few minutes later, the train arrived slowly. I got on, found my seat, and placed my bag above me. Luckily, I had a seat near the window, which made me happy because I enjoy watching the landscape during long journeys.

As the train started moving, the city slowly disappeared behind us. Soon, I could see fields, forests, small villages, and rivers outside the window. The weather was beautiful, with sunlight shining across the countryside.
For the first hour, I mostly listened to music and relaxed. Some passengers around me were sleeping, while others were quietly talking or working on laptops.
At one stop, an older man sat next to me.
“Is this seat free?” he asked politely.
He smiled and thanked me before sitting down.

Later, I opened my book and started reading. The story was interesting, and time passed more quickly than I expected. From time to time, I stopped reading just to look outside again. The changing landscape made the journey feel peaceful and relaxing.

The train stopped many times during the trip. Sometimes people got off, and new passengers entered. At one station, a family with young children sat nearby, and the children looked very excited about traveling by train.
Around lunchtime, I bought a sandwich and tea from the small café area inside the train. It was simple, but good enough for the journey.
After almost three hours, the train finally arrived at my destination. I stretched my arms, picked up my bag, and stepped onto the platform.
Even though I felt tired from sitting for so long, I was also very happy to arrive.
My friend was already waiting outside the station.
“There you are!” she said with a big smile.
“It was a long trip,” I answered, “but a really nice one.”
questions: Why did the person take the train?
Where did the person sit?
What did the person do during the journey?
Who sat next to the person?
How did the person feel after arriving?
answers_q: To visit a friend.
Near the window.
Listened to music and read a book.
An older man.
Tired but happy.
speaking: You are talking about a recent train trip.
example: “Yesterday, I took a train to another city. I listened to music and enjoyed the view during the trip.”
grammar: Used to describe travel experiences and completed past actions.

title: A Late Breakfast
content: Last Sunday, I woke up much later than usual. During the week, I normally wake up early for work, but this time there was no alarm, no meetings, and no schedule to follow.
When I opened my eyes, sunlight was already coming through the curtains. I looked at the clock and smiled.
“11:00 already,” I thought. “I really needed the sleep.”
I stayed in bed for a few more minutes, enjoying the quiet morning. Outside, I could hear birds and distant traffic, but everything felt calm and peaceful.
Since I did not have any plans for the day, I decided to take my time and enjoy the morning slowly. After taking a shower and getting dressed, I started thinking about breakfast.
At first, I considered preparing something at home, but then I remembered a small café near my house that I liked very much.

The weather was cool but sunny when I left the apartment. The streets were not too busy, and many people looked relaxed because it was Sunday. Some families were walking together, while others sat outside cafés drinking coffee.
When I arrived at the café, I immediately noticed the warm atmosphere inside. Soft music was playing quietly, and the smell of fresh coffee filled the room.
A waitress welcomed me with a smile.
“Good morning. Table for one?” she asked.
She showed me a small table near the window. I sat down, took off my jacket, and looked at the menu carefully.
In the end, I ordered eggs, toast, and coffee. The breakfast was simple, but it looked delicious when it arrived.
“Enjoy your meal,” the waitress said kindly.

While eating, I watched people walking outside and listened to the quiet conversations around me. After breakfast, I took out my book and started reading. Time passed slowly, and for once, I did not feel stressed or rushed.
I stayed there for almost one hour, drinking coffee and reading peacefully.
Before leaving, I looked around the café one last time and thought about how nice simple moments can be.
When I finally walked back home, I felt calm, relaxed, and ready for the rest of the day.
questions: Why did the person wake up late?
Where did the person go for breakfast?
What did the person order?
What did the person do at the café after eating?
How did the person feel after leaving the café?
answers_q: Because it was Sunday and there was no work.
To a small café near home.
Eggs, toast, and coffee.
Read a book and relaxed.
Calm and ready for the day.
cloze: The person woke up at around ______.

example: “Last Sunday, I went to a small café and had eggs, toast, and coffee for breakfast.”
grammar: I stayed at the café for one hour.
Used to describe daily activities and relaxing moments in the past.

title: A Problem with an Order
content: Yesterday evening, I ordered food online because I was too tired to cook. It had been a long day at work, and when I finally arrived home, I only wanted to relax and eat something good.
At first, I opened the refrigerator to see if I could prepare a quick meal, but there was almost nothing inside except a few drinks and some vegetables.
“No chance,” I thought while closing the door.
So I took my phone and opened a food delivery app. After looking at different restaurants for several minutes, I finally chose a small Italian restaurant near my apartment.
I ordered a pizza, garlic bread, and a drink. While waiting, I changed into comfortable clothes and watched television on the sofa.
At that moment, I was extremely hungry.
“I hope they arrive quickly,” I said to myself.
About thirty minutes later, someone rang the doorbell. I immediately stood up and opened the door.
I took the bag, thanked him, and returned to the kitchen feeling excited to eat. But when I opened the order, I suddenly stopped.
“This isn’t my food,” I said in surprise.
Inside the bag, there was sushi, soup, and rice. I checked the receipt again carefully. The name on the order was completely different.
At first, I laughed a little because the situation felt strange. But after a few seconds, I became frustrated because I was very hungry and did not want to wait again.
“Hi,” I explained politely. “I think there is a problem with my order. I received the wrong food.”
“Oh no, I’m very sorry about that,” she replied immediately.
She asked for my order number and checked the information on her computer.
“Yes, I see the mistake now,” she explained. “We will prepare your correct order and send it as quickly as possible.”
“Thank you,” I said, trying to stay patient.
This time, the waiting felt much longer. I watched part of a movie, checked my phone several times, and drank water to feel less hungry.
Finally, around forty minutes later, the doorbell rang again.
The second delivery driver smiled apologetically.
I opened the bag immediately, and this time the order was correct.
The pizza smelled amazing, and the garlic bread was still warm.
After the first bite, I smiled with relief.
The evening was more complicated than expected, but at least I finally got the right food.
By the end of dinner, I felt happy… but also a little tired from the whole situation.
questions: Why did the person order food online?
What food did the person originally order?
What was wrong with the first delivery?
How did the restaurant react?
How did the person feel at the end?
answers_q: Because the person was too tired to cook.
Pizza, garlic bread, and a drink.
It was the wrong order.
They apologized and sent a new order.
Happy but tired.

Used to describe problems and solutions in the past.

title: Meeting a New Neighbor
content: Last week, I met my new neighbor for the first time. A few days earlier, I had noticed boxes and furniture in the hallway, so I knew someone new was moving into the apartment next door.

At first, I did not really think much about it. People sometimes move in and out of the building, and not everyone talks to their neighbors. But after a few days, I became curious because I often heard sounds of moving and unpacking next door.

One evening, when I arrived home after work, I saw a man standing near the entrance with several grocery bags in his hands. He looked a little tired and was trying to open the building door at the same time.
I opened the door for him, and we entered the building together.
“Thanks,” he said. “I just moved in next door this week.”
“Oh, so you’re the new neighbor,” I replied.
Inside the hallway, he introduced himself.
He seemed very friendly and relaxed, so we continued talking for a few minutes near the stairs. He explained that he had moved because of a new job in the city.
“It’s still a little stressful,” he admitted. “I’m trying to learn everything about the area.”
I understood exactly what he meant because I also felt lost when I first moved there a few years ago.
We spoke about our jobs, daily routines, and the neighborhood. I told him where he could find a good supermarket, a pharmacy, and a nice café nearby.
At one point, he asked, “Do you like living here?”
“Yes,” I answered honestly. “It’s usually quiet, and people are friendly.”
“That’s good to hear,” he replied with a smile.
Before going back to our apartments, we talked a little more about the city and public transportation. The conversation felt natural and comfortable, even though we had only just met.
Since that evening, we often see each other in the hallway or outside the building. Now, we say hello almost every day. Sometimes we talk for a few minutes before leaving for work.
It’s nice to know the people who live nearby.
I still do not know Daniel very well yet, but my first impression was very positive.
I think he is a really nice person.
questions: Why did the person know someone new was moving in?
Where did the first conversation happen?
Why did Daniel move to the city?
What information did the person give Daniel?
How is their relationship now?
answers_q: Because there were boxes and furniture in the hallway.
Near the building entrance and stairs.
Because of a new job.
Information about shops and places nearby.
They say hello every day.

speaking: You are introducing yourself to a new neighbor.
example: “Hi, I’m Emma. I live in the apartment next door. Welcome to the building!”
grammar: Used to describe new social interactions and past conversations.

title: A Day Without Internet
content: Yesterday morning, I woke up and immediately noticed something strange. Usually, the first thing I do is check my phone for messages and news, but this time nothing loaded.
At first, I thought the problem was temporary.
I waited a few seconds and tried again, but the internet still did not work.
Then I turned on my computer because I needed to work from home that day. But once again, nothing connected. My emails would not open, websites did not load, and video calls were impossible.
Very quickly, I started to feel frustrated.
I restarted the internet box two times and checked the cables carefully. I even turned my phone off and on again, hoping the problem would disappear.
“This is going to be a long day,” I thought.
Without internet, I suddenly realized how many things depended on it. I could not work properly, watch videos, listen to online music, or even check simple information.
For almost thirty minutes, I continued trying to fix the problem. Finally, I gave up and decided to stop stressing about it.
“Well,” I said to myself, “I can’t change it right now.”
So I decided to do something different instead.
First, I made a cup of tea and started reading a book that had been sitting on my shelf for several weeks. Usually, I always said I was “too busy” to read, but suddenly I had plenty of time.
The story quickly caught my attention, and after a while I stopped thinking about the internet problem completely.

Later in the afternoon, I cleaned my apartment. I organized my desk, washed the dishes, and even cleaned the windows. Surprisingly, it felt satisfying to do simple things slowly without checking my phone every few minutes.
Around 5 o’clock, I decided to go for a walk outside.
The weather was beautiful, with clear skies and fresh air. People were sitting in cafés, children were playing in the park, and everything felt calm.
While walking, I realized something interesting: without internet, the day actually felt quieter and less stressful.
When I returned home in the evening, I checked the router one more time.
This time, the little lights were finally working normally again.
The internet was back, and everything worked perfectly again.
But strangely, after spending a whole day offline, I also felt more relaxed than usual.
questions: What problem did the person have?
Why was the person frustrated at first?
What did the person do after giving up on fixing the internet?
Where did the person go later in the day?
How did the person feel in the evening?
answers_q: The internet did not work.
Because the person could not work or watch anything.
Read a book and cleaned the house.
For a walk outside.
Happy and relaxed.
cloze: Without internet, the person could not ______ properly.

speaking: You are explaining an internet problem to a friend.
example: “My internet stopped working yesterday, so I spent the day reading and relaxing instead.”
grammar: Used to describe technology problems and daily activities in the past.

title: A Visit to a Friend
content: Last weekend, I visited a friend who lives in another city. We had not seen each other for several months because we were both busy with work and daily life. We talked often by phone, but it was not the same as meeting in person.
A few weeks earlier, my friend sent me a message.
“You should come visit one weekend,” he wrote.
So finally, I decided to go.

On Saturday morning, I woke up early and took the train to his city. The station was already busy with travelers carrying bags and drinking coffee before their trips. I bought a snack for the journey and found my seat near the window.
During the train ride, I listened to music and watched the landscape outside. The trip was calm and relaxing, and I felt excited about seeing my friend again after such a long time.
After about two hours, the train arrived at the station. As soon as I stepped onto the platform, I saw my friend waiting for me near the entrance.
“There you are!” he said with a big smile.
We hugged and immediately started talking, almost like no time had passed since our last meeting.
“I’m really happy you came,” he said.

The weather was sunny, so we decided to walk to his house instead of taking the bus. Along the way, he showed me different parts of the city and talked about new cafés, restaurants, and places that had opened recently.

His apartment was small but comfortable. After arriving, we sat in the living room and talked for hours. We discussed work, travel, family, and funny memories from university. Sometimes we laughed so much that we could hardly continue the conversation.
In the evening, we decided to go out for dinner at a restaurant near the city center. The place was lively and full of people enjoying their Saturday night.
We ordered burgers, fries, and drinks. The food was delicious, and the atmosphere was very friendly.
At one point, my friend told an embarrassing story about something that happened at work.
“You’re joking!” I said while laughing loudly.
“No, it’s true,” he replied, laughing too.
After dinner, we walked through the city for a while before returning home. Even though I felt tired from traveling, I also felt very happy and relaxed.
I stayed one night at his apartment. The next morning, we had breakfast together before I returned to the station.
When my train left, my friend waved goodbye from the platform.
“I will,” I answered with a smile.
It was a simple weekend, but a really special one.

How did the person travel to the city?
What did they do at the friend’s house?
What did they eat for dinner?
How long did the person stay?
answers_q: Because they had not seen each other for months.
By train.
They talked for hours.
Burgers and fries.
One night.

speaking: You are describing a recent visit to a friend.
example: “Last weekend, I visited a friend in another city. We had dinner together and talked a lot.”
grammar: Used to describe visits, travel, and social moments in the past.

title: A Difficult Day
content: Yesterday was not a good day at all. From the moment I woke up, everything seemed to go wrong.
Usually, I wake up at 6:30 every morning, but yesterday my alarm did not ring. When I finally opened my eyes, sunlight was already filling the room. I immediately looked at the clock and felt panic.
I jumped out of bed and got dressed as quickly as possible. I did not have time for breakfast, and I almost forgot my work bag near the door. The whole morning felt rushed and stressful.
Outside, I ran to the bus stop, hoping I could still catch my usual bus. But when I arrived, I saw it driving away at the end of the street.
“No, no, no…” I said quietly in frustration.
The next bus arrived twenty minutes later, and during the wait I kept checking the time nervously. I already knew I would arrive late at work.
When I finally entered the office, most of my colleagues were already working. My manager looked at me as soon as I walked in.
“I’m really sorry,” I replied. “My alarm didn’t ring.”
He nodded, but he did not look very happy.
That immediately made me feel worse.
I sat down at my desk and tried to focus on work, but my stress only increased during the morning. Around 11 o’clock, my manager asked me about an important task for a client.
For a few seconds, I did not know what to say.
“I… I’m sorry,” I admitted nervously. “I forgot to finish it yesterday.”
“We really need it today,” he explained.

At that moment, I felt extremely stressed and embarrassed. I immediately started working on the task as quickly as possible. I stopped checking my phone, ignored unnecessary emails, and focused only on finishing the project.
Little by little, things started improving.
By the afternoon, I finally completed the task and sent it to my manager. A few minutes later, he came to my desk.
“Thank you for finishing it quickly,” he said. “I know today has been difficult.”
Hearing that made me feel much calmer.
The rest of the afternoon passed more smoothly, and by the end of the day I finally felt more in control again.
When I arrived home in the evening, I changed into comfortable clothes, made tea, and sat quietly on the sofa. I watched a movie and tried not to think about the stressful morning anymore.
In the end, the day started badly… but it finished much better than I expected.

What happened at the bus stop?
Why was the manager unhappy?
What important thing did the person forget?
How did the day end?
answers_q: Because the alarm did not ring.
The bus had already left.

The day ended well and calmly.

speaking: You are explaining a difficult day to a friend.
example: “Yesterday was stressful because I woke up late and forgot an important task at work.”
grammar: Used to describe difficult situations and completed actions in the past.

title: A Good Movie
content: Last night, I watched a movie that my friend recommended to me a few days ago. Usually, we have very different tastes in movies, so at first I was not completely sure I would enjoy it.
“You really need to watch it,” my friend told me earlier this week. “I think you’ll love it.”
Yesterday evening, after dinner, I finally decided to give it a chance. I made tea, turned off the lights in the living room, and sat comfortably on the sofa with a blanket.
At the beginning, I still had doubts. The first few minutes were calm, and I thought the story might become boring.
But slowly, the movie became more and more interesting.

The story was simple, but very exciting. It followed two friends traveling across the country together after many years without seeing each other. Along the way, they had funny conversations, small problems, and emotional moments.
The movie was not full of action or special effects, but it felt very natural and realistic.
After about thirty minutes, I realized I was completely focused on the story.
At one point, one character said something funny, and I laughed out loud while sitting alone in my apartment.
“The actors are really good,” I thought.
Their conversations felt realistic, and the emotions seemed genuine. I especially liked the main actor because he reminded me a little of someone I knew in real life.
As the movie continued, I became even more curious about the ending. I stopped checking my phone completely and watched carefully until the final scene.
When the movie ended, I stayed on the sofa for a few seconds thinking about the story.
“That was actually excellent,” I said quietly to myself.
I honestly did not expect to enjoy it so much.
A few minutes later, I sent a message to my friend.
“You were right,” I wrote. “The movie was great!”
“I knew you would like it,” he answered.
We exchanged a few more messages about our favorite scenes and characters. Then I looked up the actors online because I wanted to know if they had played in other movies too.
Before going to sleep, I kept thinking about the story and the ending.
It was one of those movies that stays in your mind even after it finishes.
questions: Who recommended the movie?
Why was the person unsure at first?
What was the movie about?
What did the person think about the actors?
What message did the person send to the friend?
answers_q: A friend.
Because they usually liked different movies.

They were very good and realistic.
“The movie was great!”

grammar: Used to describe entertainment and opinions about past experiences.

title: A New Hobby
content: Recently, I started a new hobby: learning how to cook. Before that, I usually bought ready-made meals or ordered food online because cooking seemed too difficult and stressful for me.
But a few months ago, I realized I wanted to change my habits. I wanted to eat better, spend less money, and learn something useful at the same time.
So one evening, I decided to try cooking for myself.
At first, it was much more difficult than I expected.

The first meal I prepared was pasta with vegetables and chicken. It sounded simple, but everything went wrong. I cooked the pasta too long, burned the chicken slightly, and added too much salt to the sauce.
When I tasted it, I made a face immediately.
Even though the result was not very good, I did not want to stop. Instead, I watched cooking videos online and searched for easy recipes for beginners.
Little by little, I started learning basic things: how to cut vegetables properly, how long to cook rice, and how to use different spices.
Every evening after work, I practiced for a short time in the kitchen. Sometimes the food was excellent, and sometimes it was still a disaster.
One day, I accidentally dropped part of a soup on the floor.
Another time, I forgot the food in the oven for too long.
I started following simple recipes step by step instead of trying complicated dishes. That helped me feel more confident.
Now, I can cook much better than before.
I especially enjoy preparing pasta dishes, soups, and simple chicken recipes. Cooking no longer feels stressful. In fact, it has become relaxing for me.
I also like choosing ingredients at the supermarket and trying new flavors from time to time.
Last weekend, I invited two friends to my apartment for dinner. I was nervous because it was the first time I cooked for other people.
“What if they don’t like it?” I thought while preparing the meal.
I made chicken with rice and vegetables, plus a chocolate dessert I found online.
When we finally sat down to eat, my friends smiled after the first few bites.
“This is really good,” one friend said.
“You cooked all of this yourself?” the other asked with surprise.
Hearing those comments made me feel very happy. I still have a lot to learn, but now I truly enjoy cooking.
What started as a difficult hobby has become something I look forward to every day.
questions: Why did the person start learning how to cook?
What problems did the person have at the beginning?
How did the person improve?
What food does the person enjoy cooking now?
What did the friends think about the dinner?
answers_q: To eat better and learn something useful.
The meals did not taste good at first.
By practicing every day and following recipes.
Pasta dishes, soups, and chicken recipes.
They liked the food very much.
cloze: At first, the person made many ______.
speaking: You are talking about a hobby you recently started.
example: “Recently, I started learning how to cook. At first it was difficult, but now I really enjoy it.”
grammar: Used to describe learning experiences and hobbies in the past.

title: A Day at the Beach
content: Last summer, I went to the beach with some friends during a very hot weekend in August. We had all worked a lot during the previous weeks, so everyone wanted a relaxing day near the sea.
Early in the morning, we packed towels, drinks, sandwiches, and sunscreen into bags before leaving the city by car. The roads were already busy because many people had the same idea.
“It’s going to be crowded,” one friend said while looking at the traffic.
“Probably,” I replied, “but it will still be worth it.”

After almost two hours, we finally arrived at the beach. As soon as I stepped out of the car, I could smell the sea air and hear the sound of the waves. The sky was completely blue, and the sun was already very strong.
The weather was extremely hot, so we quickly found a place on the sand and put our things down.
“I’m going straight into the water,” my friend Lucas announced immediately.
A few seconds later, we were all running toward the sea.
The water was warm and refreshing. We swam for a long time, played in the waves, and laughed together like children. Sometimes we floated quietly on the water while watching the sky above us.

After swimming, I returned to the beach towel and lay on the warm sand. I put on my headphones, listened to music, and closed my eyes for a while. Around me, people were talking, children were building sandcastles, and music was coming from nearby speakers.
The atmosphere felt happy and relaxed.
Around lunchtime, we sat together under a beach umbrella and ate sandwiches, fruit, and cold drinks. We talked about vacations, work, and funny memories from school.
At one point, Lucas tried to open a bottle too quickly, and water spilled everywhere.
In the afternoon, we walked slowly along the beach and took pictures near the rocks. Some people were surfing, while others were playing volleyball near the water.
Little by little, the day became calmer as the sun started going down.
In the evening, we sat quietly on the sand and watched the sunset together. The sky turned orange, pink, and purple, and the reflection on the sea looked beautiful.
For a few minutes, nobody spoke very much.
By the time we returned home, I felt tired from the sun and the swimming, but also incredibly happy.
It was one of those simple summer days that you never forget.
questions: Why did the friends go to the beach?
What did they do in the sea?
What did the person do on the sand?
What happened during lunch?
What did they watch in the evening?
answers_q: Because they wanted a relaxing day.
They swam and played in the waves.
Listened to music and relaxed.
Lucas spilled water accidentally.
The sunset.

In the evening, they watched the ______.
speaking: You are describing a day at the beach to a friend.
example: “Last summer, I went to the beach with friends. We swam, ate together, and watched the sunset.”
grammar: Used to describe vacations and outdoor activities in the past.

title: A Job Interview
content: Yesterday, I had a job interview for a position at a marketing company. I had prepared for it during the entire week, but despite that, I still felt very nervous on the morning of the interview.
When I woke up, my first thought was about the meeting.
“Don’t panic,” I told myself while getting ready.
I chose my clothes carefully, checked my documents several times, and left home earlier than necessary because I did not want to risk arriving late.
During the bus ride, I reviewed possible interview questions in my head.

“What are your strengths?”
 “Why do you want this job?”
 “Tell us about your experience.”
The more I thought about the interview, the more nervous I became.
Luckily, traffic was not too bad, and I arrived at the office building almost twenty minutes early. The company was located in a modern building with large glass windows and a bright reception area.
At the reception desk, a woman smiled at me.
“Good morning. How can I help you?” she asked.
“I’m here for a job interview,” I replied.
She asked for my name and invited me to wait in a small seating area near the office entrance.
While waiting, I looked around the room and tried to relax. Several employees were walking quickly through the hallways carrying laptops and coffee cups. Everyone looked busy and professional.
A few minutes later, a man wearing a dark blue jacket came toward me.
“Hello, I’m Mr. Taylor,” he said while shaking my hand. “Please come with me.”
I followed him into a meeting room on the third floor. The room was quiet and simple, with a large table and a window overlooking the city.
At first, I felt very tense, but the interviewer spoke calmly and politely, which helped me relax a little.
He asked me questions about my previous jobs, my studies, and the type of work I enjoyed most.
I answered carefully and tried to stay calm.
Sometimes I needed a few seconds to think before answering, but overall the conversation went smoothly.
At one point, the interviewer smiled and said, “You seem very organized.”
Hearing that immediately gave me more confidence.
We also talked about teamwork, communication, and future projects in the company. The interview lasted about forty minutes, but it felt much shorter.
At the end, Mr. Taylor stood up and shook my hand again.
“Thank you for coming today,” he said. “We will contact you next week.”
“Thank you for your time,” I replied.
As soon as I left the building, I took a deep breath.
I still did not know if I would get the job, but one thing was certain: I felt relieved, calmer, and proud of myself for doing my best.
And honestly, I think the interview went well.
questions: Why was the person nervous?
Why did the person leave home early?
Where did the person wait before the interview?
What topics did they discuss during the interview?
How did the person feel after the interview?
answers_q: Because of the job interview.
To avoid arriving late.
In the office reception area.
Experience, studies, and work.
Relieved and proud.
cloze: After the interview, the person felt ______.
speaking: You are answering a simple question during a job interview.
example: “I have experience working in customer service, and I enjoy working with other people.”
grammar: Used to describe professional situations and past experiences.

title: A Delayed Flight
content: Last month, I took a flight to visit my cousin in another country. I had planned the trip for several weeks, so I was very excited on the day of departure.

I arrived at the airport early in the morning because I did not want any problems before the flight. Everything seemed normal at first. The airport was busy, but organized. People were checking screens, carrying suitcases, and waiting near the boarding gates.
After checking in my luggage, I passed through security and found my gate without difficulty.
I still had some time before boarding, so I bought a coffee and sat near the large windows overlooking the planes outside. The weather was cloudy, but nothing seemed unusual.
About thirty minutes later, an announcement suddenly came through the speakers.
“Attention passengers,” a voice said. “Flight 247 to Madrid is delayed by approximately two hours.”
At first, I thought I misunderstood.
Many passengers around me looked surprised and disappointed too. Some people immediately stood up to ask questions at the information desk.
I sighed and checked the time on my phone.
“Well, I guess I’ll be here for a while,” I thought.
At first, I felt a little frustrated and bored because I had already spent a lot of time waiting at the airport. But after a few minutes, I decided to stay calm and make the best of the situation.
I walked around the airport for some time and looked at the different shops. I visited a bookstore, watched people rushing to their gates, and listened to conversations in different languages.
Airports are strange places because everyone is going somewhere different.
Later, I bought a cold drink and something small to eat before sitting down again near the gate. While waiting, I read messages on my phone and answered a few emails.
At one point, I called my cousin.
“That’s annoying,” she replied, “but don’t worry. I’ll still be there when you arrive.”
Finally, after a long wait, another announcement came.
“Passengers for flight 247 may now begin boarding.”
Everybody immediately started gathering their bags and standing in line.

Once I entered the plane and sat down in my seat, I finally felt relaxed again. The flight itself was calm and comfortable. During the journey, I listened to music, watched part of a movie, and looked outside the window at the clouds.
Even though the delay was annoying, the trip ended well in the end.
And when I arrived, my cousin welcomed me with a big smile at the airport.

How long was the flight delay?
What did the person do while waiting?
Who did the person call?
How was the flight in the end?
answers_q: To take a flight and visit a cousin.
Two hours.
Walked around, bought a drink, and read messages.
The cousin.
Calm and comfortable.

speaking: You are talking about a delayed flight experience.
example: “My flight was delayed for two hours, so I waited at the airport and read messages on my phone.”
grammar: Used to describe travel situations and past events.

title: A New Restaurant
content: Yesterday evening, I tried a new restaurant with a friend after work. For several weeks, my friend had been talking about this place and telling me how good the food was.
“You really need to try it,” she said many times.

At first, I was not completely sure because I usually prefer going to restaurants that I already know. I do not always enjoy trying new places, especially when I do not know the menu or the atmosphere.
The restaurant was located on a small street near the city center. When we arrived, I immediately noticed the warm lights and large windows. Inside, the atmosphere looked calm and welcoming.
“Okay,” I admitted while smiling, “this place actually looks really nice.”

A waiter welcomed us at the entrance and showed us to a table near the window. The restaurant was busy, but not too noisy. Soft music was playing in the background, and the smell of fresh food filled the room.
After looking at the menu for several minutes, we decided to order different dishes so we could taste more things.
My friend chose grilled chicken with vegetables, while I ordered pasta with mushrooms and cream sauce. We also shared a small salad and drinks.
While waiting for the food, we talked about work, vacations, and weekend plans. The waiter came back several times to check if everything was okay, and he was always polite and friendly.
A few minutes later, the food arrived.
The dishes looked beautiful, and the smell was amazing.
I tasted my pasta and immediately looked at my friend.
“Wow,” I said with surprise. “This is really good.”
The food was simple, but very tasty and fresh. Even the bread served with the meal was excellent.
“So,” she asked, “do you still prefer your usual restaurant?”
After dinner, we stayed at the table for a while because the atmosphere felt relaxing. Around us, people were talking quietly, laughing, and enjoying their meals.
Before leaving, the waiter thanked us warmly and wished us a good evening.
Outside, the streets were cooler and quieter than before. As we walked home, we continued talking about the restaurant.
And honestly, I really meant it.
questions: Who recommended the restaurant?
Why was the person unsure at first?
What food did the person order?
How was the waiter?
Did the person enjoy the restaurant in the end?
answers_q: A friend.
Because the person usually prefers familiar restaurants.
Pasta with mushrooms and cream sauce.
Friendly and polite.
Yes, very much.

The waiter showed them to a ______.

speaking: You are talking about a restaurant you recently visited.
example: “Yesterday, I tried a new restaurant with a friend. The food was delicious, and the service was excellent.”
grammar: Used to describe meals, restaurants, and opinions in the past.

title: A Difficult Exercise
content: Yesterday evening, I went to the gym after work. At first, I almost decided to stay home because I felt tired and unmotivated. The weather was cold, and my sofa looked much more comfortable than exercise.
But after thinking for a few minutes, I told myself, “You’ll feel better after training.”
So I packed my sports clothes and went to the gym anyway.

When I arrived, the place was already busy. Some people were running on treadmills, others were lifting weights, and loud music was playing through the speakers. The atmosphere felt energetic and motivating.
I started with a short warm-up and then followed my usual routine. Everything was going normally until I noticed a trainer showing a new exercise to another group nearby.
The exercise looked interesting but difficult.
“I want to try that,” I thought.

The movement required balance, strength, and coordination. At first, it looked simple when the trainer demonstrated it, but once I tried it myself, I quickly realized it was much harder than expected.
During my first attempt, I completely lost my balance.
“Oh no,” I said while laughing nervously.
A few people nearby smiled politely, but I still felt slightly embarrassed.
I tried again, but once more the movement felt awkward and uncomfortable. My arms were not in the right position, and my legs moved too slowly.
For a moment, I thought about stopping.
But then I remembered why I came to the gym in the first place: to improve little by little.
Again and again, I repeated the exercise carefully. I watched other people, adjusted my posture, and listened to advice from the trainer.
“Take your time,” he told me kindly. “Focus on control, not speed.”
After some time, I finally started improving. My movements became smoother, and I could feel the difference. The exercise was still difficult, but now I understood it much better.
When I successfully completed the movement correctly for the first time, I smiled immediately.
At that moment, I felt proud because I did not give up.
By the end of the workout, my body felt extremely tired. My legs were heavy, and my arms hurt slightly. But despite the physical exhaustion, I also felt satisfied and motivated.
On the way home, I realized something important: sometimes difficult exercises are the most rewarding ones.
The experience was challenging, but it also made me more confident.
And honestly, I was already thinking about trying the exercise again next time.

What was difficult about the new exercise?
How did the person feel after the first attempts?
What advice did the trainer give?
How did the person feel at the end?
answers_q: Because the person felt tired.
It required balance and coordination.
Embarrassed and frustrated.
To focus on control, not speed.
Tired but proud and satisfied.

speaking: You are talking about a difficult workout at the gym.
example: “Yesterday, I tried a new exercise at the gym. It was difficult at first, but I improved after practicing.”
grammar: Used to describe physical activities and personal progress in the past.

title: A Rainy Afternoon
content: Yesterday afternoon, it rained a lot. When I looked outside the window after lunch, the sky was dark gray and heavy clouds covered the entire city.
A few minutes later, the rain started suddenly.
At first, I thought it would stop quickly, but the rain became stronger and stronger. Water ran down the windows, and I could hear the sound of the rain hitting the streets and rooftops outside.
Because of the weather, I decided to stay at home.
Normally, I like going outside during the afternoon, especially for a walk or coffee, but yesterday the weather made everything feel slow and quiet.
At first, I felt a little bored.
I walked around my apartment without knowing what to do. I checked my phone several times, opened the refrigerator even though I was not hungry, and looked outside again.
“It’s going to rain all day,” I thought.
For a moment, I considered working or cleaning the apartment, but honestly I did not feel motivated. So instead, I decided to make the afternoon more relaxing.
I chose a movie that one of my friends recommended recently. It was a mystery film with a simple story but interesting characters.
I sat comfortably on the sofa with a blanket and started watching.
Little by little, the movie completely caught my attention. The story became more exciting, and soon I forgot about the rain outside.
After the movie ended, I went to the kitchen and made some hot tea. The apartment smelled warm and comfortable while the rain continued outside.
Then I carried my tea to the living room and sat near the window.
For several minutes, I simply listened to the sound of the rain.
Cars passed slowly through the wet streets below, people walked quickly with umbrellas, and the lights from nearby buildings reflected on the pavement.
The atmosphere felt calm and peaceful.
I realized that sometimes rainy afternoons can actually be relaxing instead of boring.
I spent the rest of the afternoon quietly reading a few pages of my book and listening to music in the background.
By the evening, the rain became lighter, and the city outside looked calmer than before.
Before going to bed, I thought about the day and smiled.
At first, I expected a boring afternoon.
But in the end, it became one of the most relaxing moments of the week.
questions: Why did the person stay at home?
How did the person feel at first?
What did the person decide to watch?
Where did the person drink tea?
How did the afternoon feel in the end?
answers_q: Because it was raining heavily.
Bored.
A mystery movie.
Near the window.
Calm and relaxing.
speaking: You are describing a relaxing rainy day at home.
example: “Yesterday afternoon, it rained a lot, so I stayed home, watched a movie, and drank tea.”
grammar: Used to describe weather situations and relaxing past activities.

title: A Busy Weekend
content: Last weekend, I was very busy from beginning to end. Usually, I try to rest during the weekend, but this time I had many things to do and several people to see.
On Saturday morning, I woke up early and immediately started cleaning my apartment. During the previous week, I had been very busy with work, so everything at home felt disorganized.
There were clothes on the chair, dishes in the kitchen, and papers everywhere on my desk.
“I really need to clean this place,” I thought.
I opened the windows, turned on some music, and started cleaning room by room. I washed the dishes, vacuumed the floor, organized my desk, and changed the bedsheets.
The work took much longer than expected.
By lunchtime, I already felt tired, but I was happy because the apartment finally looked clean again.
After a short break, I went shopping in the afternoon. First, I visited a supermarket to buy food for the week: vegetables, pasta, fruit, bread, and drinks.
The supermarket was crowded, and many people were shopping at the same time. At one point, I waited almost fifteen minutes in line at the cashier.
After that, I went to a clothing store because I also needed a few new clothes. I bought a simple jacket and two shirts for work.
When I finally returned home, I only had enough time to change clothes before going out again.
In the evening, I met some friends at a restaurant near the city center. We had not seen each other for several weeks, so everyone had many things to talk about.
The restaurant was lively and noisy, but the atmosphere was fun. We had dinner together, laughed a lot, and shared stories about work, vacations, and daily life.
One friend told such a funny story that everybody at the table started laughing loudly.
“Only you could have that kind of problem,” someone joked.
By the time I arrived home that night, I was already exhausted.
But the weekend was not finished yet.

On Sunday morning, I visited my family. My parents invited me for lunch, and several relatives were also there. The house smelled wonderful because my mother was cooking traditional food in the kitchen.
We spent hours talking, eating, drinking coffee, and discussing family news. My younger cousin also showed me pictures from a recent trip abroad.
The afternoon passed very quickly because the atmosphere felt warm and comfortable.
When I finally returned home Sunday evening, I sat down on the sofa and immediately felt how tired my body was.
It had been a very busy weekend.
But honestly, it was also a really good one because I spent time with friends, family, and finally organized my life a little better.
questions: Why did the person clean the apartment?
What did the person buy while shopping?
Who did the person meet on Saturday evening?
What did the person do on Sunday?
How did the person feel Sunday night?
answers_q: Because the apartment was disorganized.
Food and clothes.
Friends.
Visited family and had lunch together.
Very tired.
cloze: On Saturday, the person cleaned the ______.
speaking: You are describing your weekend activities.
example: “Last weekend, I cleaned my apartment, went shopping, and visited my family.”
grammar: Used to describe weekend routines and social activities in the past.

title: A Lost Phone
content: Yesterday afternoon, I lost my phone for a short time, and it was one of the most stressful moments of the week.
After work, I decided to stop at a small café near my office before going home. I often go there because the atmosphere is calm, and the coffee is very good.
The café was not too crowded when I arrived. Soft music was playing in the background, and several people were working quietly on laptops near the windows.
I ordered a coffee and sat down at a table near the back of the room. While drinking my coffee, I checked messages on my phone, answered a few emails, and read the news online.
After about thirty minutes, I finished my drink and prepared to leave. I paid at the counter, thanked the waiter, and walked outside.
But after walking for almost five minutes, I suddenly wanted to check my phone again.
I put my hand in my pocket.
Then I checked my bag quickly.
My heart started beating faster because I realized my phone was missing. I tried checking every pocket again, but the phone was definitely not there.
I immediately thought about the café.
“I probably left it on the table,” I thought nervously.
Without wasting time, I quickly walked back to the café. During the walk, I felt more and more worried. My phone contained photos, messages, work information, and many important contacts.
When I entered the café again, I looked around immediately.
“I think I left my phone here,” I explained. “It’s black, and I was sitting near the back.”
For a few seconds, I felt extremely nervous. Then the waiter smiled and held up my phone.
“Yes! That’s my phone!” I replied with relief.
Apparently, I had left it on the table when I stood up to leave. Luckily, the waiter noticed it before anyone else sat there.
“Thank you so much,” I said. “I was really worried.”
“No problem. It happens all the time.”
After leaving the café again, I checked my pocket several times while walking home.
By the evening, the whole situation already felt a little funny.
But at the moment it happened, it was definitely stressful.

What did the person do in the café?
When did the person realize the phone was missing?
Who found the phone?
How did the person feel after finding it?
answers_q: In a café.
Drank coffee and checked messages.
After leaving the café.

Very relieved.

example: “Excuse me, I think I left my phone here. Did anyone find it?”
grammar: I went back to the café.
Used to describe problems and completed actions in the past.

title: A Helpful Stranger
content: Yesterday afternoon, I needed help while trying to find a place in the city. I had an appointment in a neighborhood that I did not know very well, and unfortunately my phone battery was almost empty.
At first, I thought I could find the address easily by myself.
But after walking for several minutes, I realized I was completely lost.
The streets all looked similar, and I could not find the building I was looking for. I checked my phone again, but the map stopped working because the battery finally died.
For a moment, I felt stressed because I did not want to arrive late. Many people were walking quickly around me, and I was not sure who to ask for help.
Finally, I noticed a man standing near a bus stop reading a newspaper. He looked calm and friendly, so I decided to speak to him.
“Excuse me,” I asked politely, “could you help me? I’m looking for Green Street.”
The man immediately looked up and smiled kindly.
He listened carefully while I explained where I needed to go. Then he pointed toward the next street and started giving directions slowly and clearly.
“First, go straight for two blocks,” he explained. “Then turn left near the pharmacy. After that, you’ll see a small park. The building is just across from it.”
Because he spoke slowly, I understood everything easily.
To make sure I remembered correctly, I repeated the directions quietly to myself.
“Straight, left at the pharmacy, then near the park,” I said.
“Yes, exactly,” the man answered with a smile.
Before leaving, I thanked him sincerely.
Then I followed his instructions carefully. As I walked through the streets, I looked for the places he mentioned.
A few minutes later, I saw the pharmacy.
“Okay, good,” I thought. “I’m going the right way.”
Soon after that, I noticed the small park, just like he described. Across from it was the exact building I needed.
I stopped for a second and smiled with relief.
Without that stranger’s help, I probably would have spent another hour walking around the city.
When I finally arrived at my appointment, I still had a few minutes before it started.
The whole experience reminded me that small acts of kindness can really change someone’s day.
And honestly, I felt very grateful for his help.

What problem did the person have with the phone?
Where was the man standing?
How did the man explain the directions?
How did the person feel at the end?
answers_q: Because the person was lost.
The battery died.

Grateful and relieved.

example: “Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to Green Street, please?”
grammar: Used to describe navigation and past interactions.

title: A Good Book
content: Last week, I started reading a book that my friend gave me for my birthday. Usually, I do not read very often because I am busy with work and other activities, but this time I decided to make more time for it.
“This book is perfect for you,” my friend told me when she gave it to me.
At first, I was not completely sure because the cover looked simple and the title did not immediately catch my attention. Still, I wanted to give it a chance.
On the first evening, I made some tea, sat comfortably on the sofa, and opened the book for the first time.

The beginning of the story was calm and easy to follow. It was about a young woman moving to a new city and trying to start a different life. At first, nothing very exciting happened, so I read slowly and only finished a few chapters.
“It’s nice,” I thought, “but maybe a little too simple.”
But after a while, the story became much more interesting.
The main character started meeting new people, discovering secrets about the city, and facing difficult decisions. Slowly, I became curious about what would happen next.
Every evening after work, I continued reading for one or two hours. Sometimes I planned to read only a few pages, but then I became so interested that I continued much longer than expected.
One night, I looked at the clock and realized it was already midnight.
“Just one more chapter,” I told myself.
Of course, I ended up reading three more chapters instead.
I especially liked the way the author described the characters. They felt realistic, and their conversations sounded natural. Some scenes were emotional, while others were funny and relaxing.
By the second day, I already wanted to know how the story would end.

Would the main character stay in the city?
 Would she finally find happiness?
 Would everything work out in the end?
I kept thinking about those questions during the day.
Finally, on the third evening, I finished the last chapter. When I closed the book, I stayed quiet for a few moments because I was honestly sad that the story was over.
The ending was emotional but hopeful, and it made me smile.
The next morning, I immediately sent a message to my friend.
“You were right,” I wrote. “I loved the book.”
Now I understand why so many people enjoy reading. A really good book can make you forget everything else for a while.
And honestly, I would definitely recommend this one too.
questions: Who gave the book to the person?
What was the story about?
When did the story become more interesting?
How long did it take to finish the book?
Did the person enjoy the ending?
answers_q: A friend.
A young woman moving to a new city.
After the beginning of the story.
Three days.
Yes, very much.

The person decided to ______ the book.
speaking: You are talking about a book you enjoyed reading.
example: “Last week, I read a very interesting book. The story became exciting after a few chapters.”

Used to describe reading experiences and opinions in the past.

title: A First Day at School
content: Yesterday was my first day at a new school, and I felt nervous from the moment I woke up. Starting in a new place is never easy, especially when you do not know anyone yet.
In the morning, I spent a long time choosing my clothes and preparing my bag. I checked everything twice because I did not want to forget anything important.
Before leaving home, my mother smiled at me and said, “Don’t worry. Everything will be fine.”
I left home earlier than necessary because I was afraid of arriving late on my first day. During the bus ride, I kept thinking about the new school, the teachers, and the other students.
“What if nobody talks to me?” I wondered.
When I arrived, several students were already standing outside the building. Some were talking loudly with friends, while others were checking their phones or looking for their classrooms.
The school building looked much bigger than my old one.
For a few moments, I felt even more nervous.
Inside, I followed the signs carefully until I found my classroom. When I entered, a few students looked at me and smiled politely.
“Hi,” one girl said. “Are you new here?”
“I’m Emma,” she said. “You can sit next to us if you want.”
That immediately made me feel more comfortable.
Soon, I started talking with several classmates. They asked me where I came from, what subjects I liked, and if I enjoyed sports or music.
Everyone seemed friendly and relaxed, which helped me calm down.
A few minutes later, the teacher entered the classroom.
The teacher introduced himself and explained how the school year would work. He talked about the lessons, homework, schedules, and class projects.
I listened carefully because I wanted to understand everything correctly.
Sometimes I took notes in my notebook so I would remember important information later.
During lunch break, Emma and two other students invited me to sit with them in the cafeteria.
“You’ll learn everything quickly,” one student told me. “The first day is always stressful.”
I laughed softly because she was absolutely right.
By the end of the afternoon, I already felt much more relaxed than in the morning. The school no longer seemed so intimidating, and I was happy that I had started talking to new people.
When I returned home, my family immediately asked, “How was your first day?”
And deep down, I knew it was a very positive beginning.
questions: Why was the person nervous?
Why did the person leave home early?
Who spoke to the person first in class?
What did the teacher explain?
How did the person feel at the end of the day?
answers_q: Because it was the first day at a new school.
Because the person did not want to arrive late.
Emma.
The lessons, homework, and schedules.
More relaxed and happy.

At the end of the day, the person felt ______.
speaking: You are introducing yourself to new classmates.
example: “Hi, my name is Alex. I’m new here, and I’m happy to meet everyone.”
grammar: Used to describe school experiences and first impressions in the past.

title: A Walk After Work
content: Yesterday evening, after work, I decided to go for a walk before returning home. The day at the office had been long and stressful, and I felt tired after spending hours in front of my computer.
My head was full of emails, meetings, and unfinished tasks.
“I need some fresh air,” I thought while leaving the building.
Usually, after work, I go directly home and rest on the sofa. But yesterday, the weather was so pleasant that I wanted to stay outside a little longer.
The sky was completely clear, and the air felt cool and comfortable after the heat of the afternoon. The sun was slowly going down, and the light in the streets looked soft and peaceful.
At first, I walked without thinking too much about where I was going. I simply followed the sidewalks slowly while listening to music through my headphones.
The city felt different in the evening.

Some people were leaving work like me, others were sitting outside cafés, and several families were walking together with children. Cars passed slowly through the streets, but the atmosphere still felt calm.
As I continued walking, I started feeling more relaxed little by little.
The stress from work slowly disappeared.
I passed by a small park where people were jogging or sitting on benches talking quietly. Near a bakery, I could smell fresh bread and pastries coming from inside.
For a moment, I thought about buying something sweet, but I decided to continue walking instead.
The music playing in my headphones also helped me relax. Some songs were calm and soft, which matched the peaceful atmosphere around me perfectly.
At one point, I stopped near a bridge and looked at the city lights reflecting on the water below. The view was beautiful, and for several seconds I simply stood there quietly.
“It’s nice to slow down sometimes,” I thought.
Normally, my days move very quickly, and I rarely take time to enjoy simple moments like this.
After about thirty minutes, I started feeling pleasantly tired from walking. My mind felt calmer, and my body felt lighter than before.
Finally, I turned toward home and walked back through the quiet streets.
When I arrived at my apartment, I immediately noticed the difference. Earlier, I had felt stressed and exhausted.
Now, I felt relaxed, peaceful, and ready to enjoy the rest of the evening quietly.
Sometimes, a simple walk is enough to completely change your mood.
questions: Why did the person go for a walk?
What was the weather like?
What did the person listen to while walking?
What did the person see near the bridge?
How did the person feel after the walk?
answers_q: Because the person needed fresh air and relaxation.
Clear and pleasant.
Music.
City lights reflecting on the water.
Relaxed and peaceful.
cloze: The person listened to ______ while walking.
After the walk, the person felt very ______.
speaking: You are describing an evening walk after work.
example: “Yesterday, I went for a walk after work because I wanted to relax and get some fresh air.”
grammar: Used to describe relaxing activities and feelings in the past.

title: A Long Queue
content: Yesterday evening, I went to the supermarket after work because I needed to buy a few things for dinner and breakfast. I was tired and wanted to return home quickly, so I planned to spend only a few minutes there.
“I’ll just buy a few things and leave,” I thought while entering the shop.
At first, everything seemed normal. I took a basket and walked through the supermarket looking for what I needed: bread, milk, eggs, fruit, and pasta.
The supermarket was busier than usual, but I did not pay much attention at the beginning.
After collecting everything, I walked toward the cashiers.
That was when I saw the problem.
Almost every cashier had a large line of customers waiting with full shopping carts. Some people looked tired, others looked impatient, and several children were crying or complaining loudly.
I only had a few items in my basket, but there was no special line for small purchases. So I chose the shortest queue and started waiting.
At first, I felt very impatient.

The line moved slowly, and every few minutes someone in front of me seemed to have another problem. One customer forgot an item, another needed help with payment, and one cashier had to call a manager.
For a moment, I even considered leaving the supermarket without buying anything.
But I was already there, and I really needed the food.
So instead, I tried to stay calm. I took out my phone and started reading messages while waiting. I also checked social media and answered a few emails.
Little by little, time passed more easily.

Around me, I could hear conversations, shopping carts moving, and the sound of products scanning at the cashiers. A woman behind me started talking about the weather with another customer, while a little boy near the candy section asked his mother for chocolate several times.
Finally, after what felt like a very long time, the queue started moving faster.
Soon, it was finally my turn.
“Good evening,” I replied while placing my items on the counter.
A few minutes later, I paid, packed my shopping into a bag, and left the supermarket.
When I stepped outside, I immediately felt relieved.
The fresh evening air felt wonderful after standing inside for so long.
Even though the queue was frustrating, I was happy to finally go home and relax.

What problem did the person see near the cashiers?
How did the person feel at first?
What did the person do while waiting?
How did the person feel after leaving the supermarket?

The queues were very long.
Impatient.
Checked the phone and read messages.
Relieved and relaxed.

grammar: I waited for a long time.
Used to describe shopping experiences and past actions.

title: A Small Repair
content: Last week, my bike suddenly broke while I was riding home from work. Usually, I use my bike almost every day because it is faster than the bus and more relaxing than driving in traffic.
The weather was nice, and I was riding slowly through a quiet street near my apartment. Then, suddenly, I heard a strange noise coming from the back wheel.
A few seconds later, the bike became difficult to control. I stopped immediately and looked at it carefully.
The chain had come off, and one part near the wheel looked damaged.
I tried to fix it myself for a few minutes, but nothing worked. My hands became dirty, and the bike still would not move correctly.
“This is impossible,” I said with frustration.
Since I could not ride the bike anymore, I walked home while pushing it beside me. The next morning, I decided to take it to a small repair shop near the city center.
The shop was full of different bikes hanging on the walls and standing near the entrance. Tools and bike parts were everywhere, and the smell of oil filled the room.
A worker wearing blue overalls came toward me.
“Hello,” he said kindly. “What seems to be the problem?”
“My bike started making strange noises yesterday,” I explained. “Now the chain doesn’t work properly.”
He examined the bike carefully for several minutes. He turned the pedals slowly, checked the wheel, and looked closely at the chain.
“It’s not serious,” he explained. “The chain moved out of place, and one small part needs replacing.”
Hearing that made me feel relieved immediately.
The worker told me the repair would take about one hour. Since the weather was pleasant, I decided to walk around the neighborhood while waiting.
I bought a coffee, visited a bookstore nearby, and sat for a few minutes in a small park.
Exactly one hour later, I returned to the repair shop.
The worker smiled when he saw me.
He showed me the repaired chain and explained what he fixed. Then he tested the bike quickly outside the shop to make sure everything worked correctly.
I paid for the service and thanked him for his help.
When I rode the bike home later that day, it felt smooth and quiet again, almost like new.
I was very satisfied with the repair and happy that the problem was not serious after all.
questions: What problem did the person have with the bike?
Why could the person not fix the bike alone?
Where did the person take the bike?
How long did the repair take?
How did the person feel afterward?
answers_q: The chain and wheel had a problem.
Because the bike still did not work properly.
To a repair shop.
One hour.
Satisfied and relieved.
