example: “My bike is making strange noises, and the chain does not work correctly.”
grammar: Used to describe mechanical problems and repairs in the past.

title: A Late Dinner
content: Yesterday, I had dinner much later than usual because work was very busy. Normally, I finish around 6 o’clock and eat dinner at a reasonable time, but yesterday everything took longer than expected.
In the afternoon, my manager gave me an urgent task that needed to be finished before the next morning.
“Can you complete this today?” he asked.
I looked at the amount of work and immediately understood that I would probably leave the office late.

For the rest of the afternoon, I stayed focused on my computer and continued working without many breaks. Several colleagues left the office one after another while I was still answering emails and checking documents.
At one point, I looked outside and realized it was already dark.
“I can’t believe it’s this late already,” I thought.
Finally, around 9 o’clock, I finished the last task and shut down my computer. By then, I felt tired, hungry, and mentally exhausted.
The streets outside were much quieter than usual when I walked home. Most shops were already closed, and only a few people were still outside.
When I arrived home, the apartment felt calm and silent.
I opened the refrigerator, hoping to find something easy to prepare, but there was almost nothing inside except a few drinks and vegetables.
At that moment, cooking felt impossible.
“I definitely don’t want to cook tonight,” I said to myself.
So instead, I ordered food online from a nearby restaurant. I chose noodles, chicken, and a drink because I wanted something warm and comforting after such a long day.
When the doorbell rang, I immediately stood up from the sofa.
“Perfect timing,” I said while taking the bag from the delivery driver.
The food smelled delicious, and I suddenly realized how hungry I really was.

I turned on the television, sat comfortably on the sofa, and started eating while watching a movie. The combination of warm food, quiet music from the TV, and finally being at home made me feel much more relaxed.
The food was simple, but very good.
After dinner, I stayed on the sofa for a while longer, watching television and slowly forgetting about the stressful workday.
Eventually, I looked at the clock and realized it was already very late.
I felt tired, but also calm and comfortable.
Before going to bed, I thought, “Tomorrow will hopefully be less busy.”
And honestly, after such a long day, that late dinner felt completely worth it.
questions: Why did the person eat dinner late?
What did the manager ask the person to do?
Why did the person not want to cook?
What food did the person order?
What did the person do after dinner?
answers_q: Because work finished very late.
To complete an urgent task.
Because the person was tired and hungry.
Noodles, chicken, and a drink.
Relaxed and watched TV.
cloze: The apartment was very ______ when the person arrived home.
After dinner, the person went to bed ______.
speaking: You are talking about a late dinner after work.
example: “Yesterday, I worked late, so I ordered food and ate while watching TV.”
grammar: Used to describe evening routines and completed past actions.

title: A Meeting Change
content: Yesterday, I had an important meeting planned for the afternoon at work. I spent most of the morning preparing documents, checking information, and organizing my notes because I wanted everything to go smoothly.
The meeting was supposed to start at 2 o’clock, and several colleagues from different departments were going to attend.
Since it was an important discussion, I felt a little stressed.
Around lunchtime, I reviewed my presentation one last time while eating quickly at my desk.
“I hope everything goes well,” I thought.
Then, a few minutes before 1 o’clock, my phone suddenly rang. It was my colleague Sarah.
“Hi,” she said. “I wanted to let you know that the meeting time changed.”
I looked at the clock immediately.
“Yes,” she explained. “One manager is stuck in another meeting, so ours will start later.”
At first, I was surprised because nobody had mentioned any changes earlier. For a second, I worried that the schedule change would create problems for the rest of the afternoon.
“What time is the new meeting?” I asked.
Luckily, after thinking about it for a moment, I realized the situation was actually not a problem at all.
In fact, the extra time was useful.
I still had several small tasks to finish before the meeting anyway. So instead of stressing about the delay, I decided to use the time productively.

I answered emails, updated a few documents, and corrected some mistakes in my presentation slides. I also had enough time to prepare coffee and take a short break, which helped me feel calmer and more organized.
By the time the meeting finally started, I actually felt much more confident than before.
When I entered the meeting room, several colleagues were already there talking quietly.
“Sorry about the change,” one manager said. “Thank you for waiting.”
The meeting itself went very well. Everyone stayed focused, the discussion was productive, and my presentation was clearer than I expected because I had extra time to prepare.
At one point, Sarah smiled and whispered quietly, “Good thing we had more preparation time.”
I laughed softly because she was absolutely right.
The meeting finished around 5 o’clock, and everyone seemed satisfied with the results.
When I left the office later that evening, I realized something important: sometimes unexpected changes can actually improve the situation instead of making it worse.
By the end of the day, I felt calm, productive, and happy with how everything turned out.
questions: What was the person preparing for?
Who called the person?
Why was the meeting delayed?
What did the person do during the extra time?
How did the meeting go in the end?
answers_q: An important meeting.
Sarah, a colleague.
Because a manager was in another meeting.
Finished work and prepared more.
It went very well.
cloze: The meeting was planned for the ______.
Sarah called to explain the meeting ______.
The person used the extra time to finish some ______.
At the end of the day, the person felt very ______.
speaking: You are informing a colleague that a meeting time changed.
example: “Hi, the meeting will start later today because the manager is still busy.”
grammar: I used the extra time to work.
Used to describe schedule changes and workplace situations in the past.

title: A Visit to the Market
content: Last Saturday morning, I went to the local market near the city center. I usually shop at the supermarket during the week because it is faster, but sometimes I prefer going to the market during the weekend.
The atmosphere feels more lively and relaxed there.
The weather was sunny and pleasant when I left home. As I walked toward the market, I could already hear people talking, laughing, and calling customers from the different stands.
When I arrived, the market was already busy.

There were colorful stands everywhere selling fruit, vegetables, bread, flowers, cheese, fish, and many other products. Some sellers were arranging their products carefully, while others were speaking with customers.
I walked slowly through the market and looked at everything around me.
The fruit looked especially fresh and colorful. There were bright red strawberries, yellow bananas, oranges, peaches, and green apples stacked neatly on the stands.
The smell of fresh bread and spices filled the air.
I stopped at several stands to compare prices before buying anything. Luckily, many products were cheaper than at the supermarket, which made me happy.
At one stand, I bought tomatoes, carrots, salad, and potatoes from an older seller wearing a large straw hat.
“Everything is fresh today,” he said proudly.
“Yes, it looks very good,” I replied.
While he prepared my vegetables, we talked for a few minutes about the weather and cooking. He was very friendly and smiled often.
Then I visited another stand to buy fruit. I chose strawberries, apples, and some peaches for dessert.
One seller offered me a small piece of melon to taste.
She was right. The melon was delicious.

As I continued walking around the market, I enjoyed the atmosphere more and more. Musicians were playing nearby, children were running between the stands, and people were carrying large shopping bags full of food.
The whole place felt alive and cheerful.
After finishing my shopping, I walked home slowly with my bags full of fresh ingredients.
When I arrived home, I immediately started cooking lunch. I prepared roasted vegetables, rice, and chicken using the ingredients from the market.
The meal turned out really well.
While eating, I realized that food often tastes better when the ingredients are fresh.
By the end of the afternoon, I felt relaxed, satisfied, and happy that I decided to visit the market instead of the supermarket.
questions: Why did the person go to the market?
What products did the person buy?
How were the prices at the market?
What was the seller like?
What did the person do after returning home?
answers_q: To buy fresh food.
Fruit and vegetables.
They were good.
Friendly.
Cooked a meal.
speaking: You are talking about shopping at a market.
example: “Last Saturday, I went to the market and bought fresh fruit and vegetables.”
grammar: Used to describe shopping activities and food preparation in the past.

title: A Missed Call
content: Yesterday afternoon, I missed a call from one of my close friends because I did not hear my phone ring. I was busy cleaning my apartment and listening to music at the same time, so I completely ignored the sound of my notifications.
At first, I did not notice anything unusual.
I spent almost one hour organizing my desk, washing dishes, and putting clothes away. The music was quite loud, and my phone was charging in another room.
Later, when I finally sat down on the sofa to rest, I picked up my phone and immediately saw several missed notifications on the screen.

One missed call.
 One voice message.
 Two text messages.
“Oh,” I said quietly. “I didn’t hear anything.”
The call was from my friend Daniel.
I quickly listened to the voice message.
“Hey!” he said cheerfully. “Call me back when you can. I have an idea for tonight.”
I smiled immediately because Daniel often makes spontaneous plans at the last minute.
Without waiting too long, I called him back.
“Sorry,” I replied. “My phone was in another room, and I didn’t hear it.”
“No problem,” he said. “Are you free this evening?”
We talked for a few minutes while he explained his idea. He wanted to meet in the city center for dinner and maybe walk around afterward because the weather was beautiful.
We agreed to meet at 7:30 near a small restaurant we both liked. After the call ended, I quickly finished cleaning the apartment and started getting ready.
In the evening, the city felt lively and pleasant. Many people were outside enjoying the warm weather, and the streets were full of lights and conversations.
When I arrived at the restaurant, Daniel was already waiting near the entrance.
“You’re on time this time,” he joked with a smile.
We had dinner together, talked about work and daily life, and shared funny stories from the week. After dinner, we walked slowly through the city center while continuing our conversation.
The evening felt simple, calm, and enjoyable.
At one point, I thought about the missed call again.
If I had not checked my phone later, I probably would have stayed home all evening.
Instead, I spent a really nice moment with a good friend.
Sometimes, even a missed call can lead to a great evening.
questions: Why did the person miss the call?
Who called the person?
What did Daniel want to do?
Where did they meet?
How was the evening?
answers_q: Because the phone was in another room.
Daniel.
Meet for dinner and walk in the city.
Near a restaurant in the city center.
Nice and enjoyable.
cloze: After dinner, they went for a ______.
speaking: You are calling a friend back after missing a call.
example: “Hi, sorry I missed your call. I didn’t hear my phone earlier.”
grammar: Used to describe communication and social plans in the past.

title: A Rainy Trip
content: Last week, I traveled to another city for a short vacation. I had planned the trip for several weeks and imagined spending most of my time walking outside, visiting famous places, and taking pictures.
Unfortunately, the weather was terrible almost the entire time.
When I arrived on the first day, dark clouds already covered the sky. A few minutes later, heavy rain started falling, and the streets quickly became wet and crowded with people carrying umbrellas.
“This is not a great start,” I thought while looking outside the train station.
At first, I hoped the rain would stop later in the day. But it continued for hours.
The next morning was exactly the same.

Because of the weather, I could not do many outdoor activities that I originally planned. I wanted to visit parks, walk near the river, and explore the old part of the city, but spending hours outside in the rain did not sound enjoyable.
Still, I did not want to stay in my hotel room all day.
So instead, I changed my plans and focused on indoor activities.
On the second day, I visited a large museum near the city center. The building was beautiful, quiet, and warm compared to the cold streets outside.

I spent several hours there looking at paintings, old photographs, and historical objects. Some exhibitions were especially interesting because they explained the history of the city and the people who lived there many years ago.
I learned many new things that I did not know before.
Later, when the rain became even stronger, I entered a small café to rest.
The café was cozy and smelled of coffee and fresh pastries. Soft music played quietly in the background while people talked calmly around me.
I ordered coffee and sat near the window with my book.
For almost one hour, I simply read, drank coffee, and watched the rain outside.

During the rest of the trip, I visited several cafés like that. Some were modern and busy, while others were small and quiet. In each place, I spent time reading, writing messages, or simply relaxing.
Of course, the trip was not exactly what I imagined at first.
The weather made many things difficult, and sometimes I felt disappointed when I looked outside and saw more rain.
But slowly, I realized something important: a trip does not need to be perfect to be enjoyable.
Even though I could not do everything I planned, I still discovered new places, learned interesting things, and enjoyed moments of calm that I normally do not have in daily life.
In the end, I still enjoyed the trip very much.
And honestly, the rainy atmosphere made the city feel unique in its own way.
questions: Why was the trip difficult?
What outdoor activities did the person want to do?
Where did the person go during the rain?
What did the person do in the cafés?
What did the person learn from the trip?
answers_q: Because it rained every day.
Walk in parks and explore the city.
To museums and cafés.
Read, drank coffee, and relaxed.
That a trip can still be enjoyable without perfect weather.
cloze: The weather was very ______ during the trip.
The person visited a ______ near the city center.
In the end, the person still enjoyed the ______.
speaking: You are talking about a trip with bad weather.
example: “Last week, I traveled to another city. It rained a lot, but I still enjoyed visiting museums and cafés.”
grammar: Used to describe travel experiences and weather conditions in the past.

title: A New Routine
content: Recently, I decided to change my daily routine because I often felt tired, stressed, and unorganized. For a long time, I had bad habits. I usually went to bed too late, skipped breakfast, and started work already feeling exhausted.
One evening, after another stressful day, I thought carefully about my lifestyle.
“Something needs to change,” I told myself.
So little by little, I started creating a new routine.
The first change was waking up earlier in the morning. At first, it was very difficult because I was used to sleeping later and pressing the alarm button many times.
During the first few days, I felt extremely tired when I got out of bed.
Now, I wake up almost one hour earlier than before. Instead of checking my phone immediately, I put on comfortable clothes and go outside for a short walk every morning.
At first, the walks felt strange because the streets were still quiet and calm so early in the day. But after some time, I started enjoying them very much.
The fresh morning air helps me wake up naturally, and walking before work makes my mind feel calmer.
Sometimes I listen to music or podcasts while walking. Other mornings, I simply enjoy the silence and watch the city slowly wake up around me.
After returning home, I prepare a healthy breakfast instead of drinking only coffee like before. Usually, I eat fruit, eggs, toast, or yogurt.
Because of that, I now start work with much more energy.
I also noticed that I concentrate better during meetings and feel less stressed during the day.
In the evening, I changed several habits too.

Before, I often stayed awake very late watching videos or checking social media. Now, I try to relax more quietly after dinner. Sometimes I read, listen to calm music, or prepare things for the next day.
I also go to bed earlier than before.
At first, these changes seemed small and unimportant. But after several weeks, I realized the difference was actually huge.

I sleep better.
 I feel calmer.
 I have more energy.
 And my days feel much more organized.
Of course, I still have stressful moments sometimes, but overall I feel healthier and more balanced than before.
One friend even noticed the difference recently.
“You look more relaxed these days,” she told me.
I smiled because she was completely right.
Changing my routine was not easy at first, but now I honestly think it was one of the best decisions I made this year.
questions: Why did the person change the routine?
What does the person do every morning now?
What healthy breakfast foods does the person eat?
What evening habits changed?
How does the person feel now?
answers_q: Because the person felt tired and stressed.
Goes for a walk.
Fruit, eggs, toast, or yogurt.
The person rests more and goes to bed earlier.
More relaxed and balanced.
cloze: Morning walks help the person feel ______.
The person starts work with more ______.
speaking: You are describing a healthy daily routine.
example: “Recently, I started waking up earlier and walking every morning before work.”
grammar: I go for a walk every morning.
Used to describe habits and routines in everyday life.

title: A Problem with a Train
content: Yesterday morning, I took a train to another city for a work meeting. Everything started normally, and at first the trip was calm and comfortable.
I arrived at the station early, bought a coffee, and found my seat near the window. The train left exactly on time, and I felt relaxed because I had prepared everything carefully for the meeting.
Outside, the weather was cloudy but dry. Some passengers were reading books, others were working on laptops, and a few people were sleeping quietly.
For about the first hour, the journey went perfectly.
Then suddenly, the train slowed down.
At first, nobody reacted because trains often stop briefly during trips. But after a few minutes, the train stopped completely in the middle of nowhere.
There were no buildings nearby, only fields and trees outside the windows.
People started looking around with confusion.
A few minutes later, an announcement came through the speakers, but it was not very clear.
“We are currently experiencing a technical problem,” the voice explained. “Please remain seated while we solve the situation.”
Nobody knew exactly what the problem was or how long the delay would last.
At first, I tried to stay calm. I checked my phone, answered messages, and listened to music. But after almost thirty minutes without movement, people around me started becoming nervous.
One woman looked worried because she had another train connection later that day. A businessman near me sighed loudly several times while checking his watch.
I also started feeling stressed because my meeting was important, and I knew I would probably arrive late.
“This is really bad timing,” I thought.
After almost one hour, the atmosphere inside the train became quieter and more tense. Some passengers walked up and down the aisle, while others tried asking staff members for information.
“Thank you for your patience,” the speaker said. “The problem has been solved, and the train will continue shortly.”
A few seconds later, the train slowly started moving again.
People smiled, relaxed, and started talking normally again. One passenger even joked, “Finally! I thought we would sleep here tonight.”
Even though the train arrived much later than expected, I was simply happy that the trip continued safely without any serious danger.
When I finally arrived at the station, I quickly contacted my colleagues to explain the situation.
The day did not go exactly as planned, but at least everything ended safely.
And honestly, after such a strange journey, arriving late no longer seemed like the biggest problem.
questions: Why did the person take the train?
What happened during the trip?
Why did passengers become worried?
How long did the train stop?
How did people feel when the train moved again?
answers_q: For a work meeting.
The train stopped because of a technical problem.
Because nobody knew what was happening.
Almost one hour.
Relieved and calmer.
cloze: The train stopped in the middle of ______.
speaking: You are explaining a train delay to someone.
example: “My train stopped for almost one hour because of a technical problem.”
grammar: Used to describe travel problems and reactions in the past.

title: A Nice Surprise
content: Yesterday evening, I had a very nice surprise from my friends. It started like a completely normal day, and I honestly expected to spend a quiet evening alone at home.
After work, I returned to my apartment feeling tired. The weather outside was cold and rainy, so I changed into comfortable clothes, made tea, and planned to relax for the rest of the evening.
I was thinking about watching a movie or reading a book when suddenly someone knocked on the door.
When I opened the door, I immediately froze with surprise.
Three of my friends were standing there smiling and holding bags of food and drinks.
For a few seconds, I did not know what to say.
“What are you doing here?” I asked while laughing.
One friend smiled and replied, “We knew you had a stressful week, so we wanted to spend the evening with you.”
That honestly made me very happy.
I invited them inside quickly, and soon the apartment felt completely different. A few minutes earlier, everything had been quiet and calm. Now there was music, conversation, and laughter everywhere.
My friends brought pizza, snacks, drinks, and even dessert.
“You really planned all this?” I asked with surprise.
We sat together in the living room, ate dinner, and talked for hours. Everyone shared stories about work, daily life, vacations, and funny situations from the week.
At one point, one friend told such a ridiculous story that we all started laughing uncontrollably.
I laughed so much that my stomach hurt.
Later in the evening, we played music and sang along to old songs we all knew from school. Some neighbors probably heard us, but luckily nobody complained.
The atmosphere felt warm, relaxed, and joyful.
What made the evening special was not the food or the music. It was simply the feeling of being surrounded by people who cared about me.
Before they arrived, I felt tired and slightly stressed from work.
But after spending time together, my mood completely changed.
When my friends finally left later that night, the apartment became quiet again. But this time, the silence felt peaceful instead of lonely.
Before going to bed, I smiled while thinking about the evening.
Sometimes the best moments are the ones you never expect.
And honestly, it was one of the nicest surprises I had in a long time.
questions: Why was the person surprised?
What did the friends bring?
Why did the friends visit?
What did they do together?
How did the person feel at the end?
answers_q: Because the friends arrived unexpectedly.
Food, drinks, and dessert.
Because the person had a stressful week.
Ate, talked, laughed, and listened to music.
Very happy.
cloze: The friends shouted “______!” at the door.
In the end, the person felt very ______.
speaking: You are talking about a surprise visit from friends.
example: “Yesterday, my friends surprised me by coming to my house with food and drinks.”
grammar: My friends came to my house.
Used to describe surprises and social moments in the past.

title: A Busy Airport
content: Last Friday, I went to the airport for an early morning flight. I was traveling to another city for a short business trip, so I needed to leave home before sunrise.
I woke up very early, quickly drank coffee, checked my passport and ticket several times, and ordered a taxi to the airport.
Outside, the streets were still dark and quiet. But as soon as I arrived at the airport, everything changed completely.
The place was already extremely busy.

There were people everywhere carrying suitcases, pushing carts, checking screens, and rushing toward different terminals. Some families were traveling with children, while other passengers looked tired and stressed.
For a moment, I simply stood there looking around.
“This place is crazy today,” I thought.
First, I needed to check in my luggage. Unfortunately, the line was already very long. I joined the queue and waited patiently while slowly moving forward.
Around me, people were talking in different languages, checking their phones, and drinking coffee while waiting.
After finally checking in my suitcase, I walked toward security control.
That part was even more stressful.
The security area was crowded and noisy. Airport staff repeated instructions constantly through loudspeakers:

“Please remove laptops from your bags.”
 “Liquids must stay inside clear plastic bags.”
Everyone moved slowly through the line, and it took much longer than expected.
At one point, I started worrying about time.
“What if I arrive late at the gate?” I thought nervously.
Luckily, everything went smoothly in the end. After security, I checked the departure screens carefully and finally found my gate.
When I arrived there, I immediately felt calmer.
I sat down near the window and watched planes arriving and leaving outside. The atmosphere near the gate was quieter than the rest of the airport.
I bought a bottle of water and answered a few messages while waiting for boarding.
Little by little, I started relaxing again.
A few minutes later, an announcement came through the speakers.
“Passengers for Flight 318 may now begin boarding.”
Everyone stood up, collected their bags, and slowly moved toward the gate entrance.
When I entered the plane and found my seat, I immediately felt relieved.
The stressful part of the journey was finally over.
I looked outside through the window while passengers continued boarding around me.
Even though the airport experience was busy and tiring, I also felt excited about the trip ahead.
And once the plane finally took off, I could finally relax completely.
questions: Why did the person go to the airport?
What made the airport stressful?
What happened at security?
What did the person do at the gate?
How did the person feel after boarding the plane?
answers_q: For a business trip.
The airport was crowded and busy.
The security line was very long.
Sat down and waited.
Relieved and calmer.
cloze: The person felt relieved after ______ the plane.
speaking: You are describing your experience at an airport.
example: “Last Friday, I went to a very busy airport, but everything went well in the end.”
grammar: Used to describe airport experiences and travel situations in the past.

title: A Cooking Experience
content: Yesterday evening, I decided to try cooking a completely new dish for dinner. Recently, I have been trying to cook more often instead of ordering food all the time, so I wanted to challenge myself with a different recipe.
After work, I searched online for something simple but interesting. After looking at several recipes, I finally chose a pasta dish with chicken, vegetables, and a creamy sauce.
“This doesn’t seem too difficult,” I thought confidently.
I made a shopping list, went to the supermarket, and bought all the ingredients I needed. When I returned home, I put some music on in the kitchen and started cooking.
At first, everything went very well.
I cut the vegetables carefully, cooked the chicken correctly, and followed the recipe step by step. The kitchen smelled wonderful, and I started feeling proud of myself.
“Maybe I’m becoming a good cook,” I joked quietly.
While the sauce was cooking, I checked the recipe again on my phone. That was when I reached the step about adding salt and spices.
Without paying enough attention, I added far too much salt to the sauce.
At first, I did not realize the problem.
But when I tasted the food a few minutes later, I immediately stopped chewing.
I tried adding more cream and vegetables to fix the flavor, but honestly, it still did not taste very good. The more I tried to improve it, the stranger the dish became.
For a moment, I felt disappointed.
I spent time buying ingredients, preparing everything carefully, and cooking for almost one hour. And in the end, the result was not great.
I sat down at the kitchen table and laughed a little at myself.
Even though the meal was not perfect, I still ate part of it because I was hungry. Surprisingly, after some time, the situation no longer felt so serious.
Later in the evening, I watched a few cooking videos online to understand what I should do differently next time.
I learned that it is always better to add small amounts of salt little by little instead of adding too much immediately.
That advice honestly seemed very useful.
By the end of the night, I no longer felt disappointed. Instead, I felt motivated to try again another day.
After all, learning something new always includes mistakes sometimes.
And next time, I will definitely be much more careful with the salt.
questions: What dish did the person try to cook?
Why did the person feel confident at first?
What mistake did the person make?
How did the food taste?
What did the person learn?
answers_q: A pasta dish with chicken and vegetables.
Because everything was going well.
Added too much salt.
Very salty.
To add salt little by little.
cloze: Next time, the person will be more ______.
speaking: You are talking about a cooking mistake.
example: “Yesterday, I tried a new recipe, but I added too much salt to the food.”
grammar: I tried to cook a new dish.
Used to describe cooking experiences and mistakes in the past.

title: A Visit to the Doctor Again
content: Last week, I went back to the doctor because I still did not feel well. A few days earlier, I had already visited him, and he gave me medicine for my headache and fever. At first, I thought I would recover quickly.
But unfortunately, that did not happen.
Even after taking the medicine for several days, I still felt tired and weak. I also had trouble sleeping at night, and sometimes I felt dizzy during the day.
At first, I tried to ignore the problem because I did not want to miss work again. But after another difficult morning, I finally decided to make another appointment with the doctor.
When I arrived at the clinic, the waiting room was full of people. Some patients were reading magazines quietly, while others were checking their phones or talking softly.
I sat down and waited patiently.
Honestly, I felt a little worried because I still did not understand why I was not improving.
After about twenty minutes, the doctor called my name.
“Good morning,” he said kindly. “How are you feeling now?”
Inside his office, he asked me more questions than before. He wanted to know exactly when I felt tired, how I was sleeping, what I was eating, and if I felt stressed recently.
I told him about my headaches, my lack of energy, and the stress from work during the past few weeks.
The doctor listened attentively and took notes while I spoke.
“I think your body needs more rest,” he explained. “You are probably too tired and stressed.”
Then he decided to change my medicine and gave me new advice.
He told me to sleep earlier, drink more water, avoid too much coffee, and rest more during the evening.
“You need to slow down for a few days,” he said seriously.
This time, I decided to follow his advice carefully.
For the next several days, I stayed home more, rested properly, and tried improving my daily habits. I also avoided working too late and spent more time relaxing.
Little by little, I started feeling better.
After a few days, my headaches became less frequent, my energy slowly returned, and I finally started sleeping normally again.
Now, I feel much healthier and happier.
Looking back, I realized something important: sometimes we continue working and pushing ourselves too hard without listening to our bodies.
And honestly, I am very happy that I finally decided to take proper care of myself.
questions: Why did the person return to the doctor?
What symptoms did the person still have?
What questions did the doctor ask?
What advice did the doctor give?
How did the person feel after following the advice?
answers_q: Because the person still felt sick.
Headaches, tiredness, and dizziness.
Questions about sleep, food, and stress.
To rest more and improve daily habits.
Much healthier and happier.
cloze: The person still did not feel ______.
After a few days, the person ______.
speaking: You are explaining your symptoms to a doctor.
example: “I still feel tired and have headaches, even after taking the medicine.”
grammar: I went back to the doctor.
Used to describe medical visits and health situations in the past.

title: A Day Without Plans
content: Yesterday, I had absolutely nothing planned for the day. Usually, my weekends are busy with work, shopping, meetings, or family visits, but this time everything was calm.
When I woke up in the morning, I looked at my phone and realized there were no messages, no appointments, and no important tasks waiting for me.
For a moment, it felt strange.
“What should I do today?” I thought while lying in bed.
At first, I stayed on the sofa doing almost nothing. I watched videos online, checked social media, and walked around the apartment without any real purpose.
Honestly, I felt a little bored.
The apartment was quiet, and outside the weather was cloudy, so going out did not seem very interesting either.
After some time, I decided that staying bored all day was probably not a good idea.
So I started cleaning the apartment little by little. First, I organized my desk and folded clothes that had been sitting on a chair for several days.
Then I cleaned the kitchen and washed the dishes.
Once I started moving, I actually felt more motivated.
I opened the windows to let fresh air inside and played music while cleaning. The apartment slowly started looking cleaner and more comfortable.
After finishing, I looked around proudly.
“This already feels much better,” I thought.
Around lunchtime, I became hungry and decided to cook something simple instead of ordering food. I prepared pasta with vegetables and chicken while listening to music in the kitchen.
Cooking relaxed me more than expected.
The meal turned out surprisingly good, and I enjoyed eating slowly without rushing anywhere.
Later in the afternoon, I made coffee and decided to watch a movie. I chose a light comedy because I wanted something relaxing and easy to follow.
I sat comfortably on the sofa with a blanket and watched the movie while rain started falling softly outside.
Without noticing, the day started passing much faster.
By the evening, I realized something interesting: even though the day seemed boring at first, it actually became very enjoyable.
I did not do anything extraordinary.
I simply cleaned, cooked, relaxed, and spent quiet time alone.
But sometimes, simple days are exactly what we need.
In the evening, I made tea, listened to music for a while, and prepared for bed feeling calm and relaxed.
Before sleeping, I smiled and thought:
“Today was simple… but honestly, it was a really good day.”
questions: Why was the day unusual for the person?
How did the person feel at first?
What did the person decide to clean?
What meal did the person cook?
How did the person feel in the evening?
answers_q: Because there were no plans or appointments.
Bored.
The apartment and kitchen.
Pasta with vegetables and chicken.
Relaxed and calm.
cloze: The person had no ______ for the day.
The person watched a ______ in the afternoon.
In the evening, the person felt very ______.
speaking: You are describing a quiet day at home.
example: “Yesterday, I stayed home, cleaned my apartment, cooked lunch, and watched a movie.”
grammar: Used to describe simple activities and routines in the past

title: A Good Decision
content: Last month, I made an important decision about my health and daily life. For a long time, I felt tired almost every day, especially after work. I spent too much time sitting, sleeping late, and staying inside.
Little by little, I realized that my habits were not very healthy.
One evening, after climbing stairs and feeling tired very quickly, I stopped for a moment and thought seriously about my situation.
“I really need to change something,” I told myself.
That was when I decided to start exercising more regularly.
At first, I did not want to do anything too difficult because I was not in very good shape. So I started with simple activities like walking in the morning and doing short exercises at home.
Honestly, the beginning was much harder than I expected.
After only a few minutes of exercise, I already felt tired. Sometimes my legs hurt, my breathing became difficult, and I wanted to stop immediately.
One morning, after a short workout, I lay on the sofa thinking:
“How do people do this every day?”
For a moment, I almost gave up.
But deep down, I knew that improving my health would take time. So instead of stopping, I decided to continue slowly and be patient with myself.
Every week, I tried to stay consistent.
Some days were easier than others. Sometimes I felt motivated and energetic, while other days I felt lazy and tired.
But even on difficult days, I still tried to do something small: a short walk, stretching, or simple exercises at home.
Little by little, I started noticing changes.
After a few weeks, I no longer felt exhausted after walking upstairs. My body felt stronger, and I started sleeping better at night.
I also noticed improvements in my mood and energy during the day.
One friend even commented on it recently.
Hearing that made me smile because I knew my efforts were finally showing results.
Now, exercise has become part of my normal routine. I still have difficult days sometimes, but overall I feel much more confident and motivated than before.
The most important thing I learned is that big changes do not happen immediately.
They happen slowly, one small step at a time.
Looking back now, I honestly believe that starting to exercise was one of the best decisions I made recently.
Because today, I feel stronger, healthier, and happier than before.
questions: Why did the person decide to change habits?
What exercises did the person start with?
Why was the beginning difficult?
What changes did the person notice later?
How does the person feel now?
answers_q: Because the person often felt tired and unhealthy.
Walking and simple exercises.
Because the person became tired quickly.
More energy, strength, and better sleep.
Stronger, healthier, and happier.
cloze: The person wanted to change something in their ______.
speaking: You are talking about a healthy decision you made.
example: “Last month, I started exercising more because I wanted to feel healthier and stronger.”
grammar: Used to describe personal changes and healthy habits over time.

title: Choosing a Restaurant
content: On Friday evening, Anna and Tom met in the city center after work. Both of them were tired after a long week, and they wanted to relax together before the weekend started.
The streets were busy, and many restaurants were already full of people talking and laughing.
“So,” she asked, “where do you want to eat?”
Tom adjusted his jacket and looked at the different restaurants nearby.
“I don’t know,” he answered honestly. “What do you think?”
For a few seconds, Anna thought carefully. Then she pointed toward a small Italian restaurant at the end of the street.
“I think we should go to the Italian restaurant,” she said.
Tom looked at the restaurant and immediately understood which one she meant. The place was famous for its pasta and pizza, and they had eaten there once before several months earlier.
“Why?” he asked with a small smile, even though he already knew the answer.
“Because the food is very good,” she replied.
Tom looked surprised for a moment.
“You forgot?” she asked. “Today is exactly one year since we started working together.”
For a moment, he looked thoughtful while watching people pass by in the street.
When they entered the restaurant, the atmosphere immediately felt warm and comfortable. Soft music played quietly in the background, and the smell of fresh pizza and pasta filled the air.
A waiter welcomed them and showed them to a table near the window.
“This place still smells amazing,” Tom said while opening the menu.

They ordered food, drinks, and dessert while talking about work, vacations, and funny memories from the past year. Sometimes they laughed loudly, and sometimes they simply enjoyed the calm atmosphere around them.
The food was excellent, just as Anna remembered.
At one point, Tom looked around the restaurant and smiled.
“You were right,” he admitted. “This was a good idea.”
After dinner, they stayed a little longer because neither of them wanted the evening to end too quickly.
Outside, the city lights reflected on the wet streets after a short rain earlier in the evening.
When they finally left the restaurant, both of them felt relaxed and happy.
It was a simple evening, but also a very memorable one.
questions: Where did Anna and Tom meet?
Which restaurant did Anna suggest?
Why did Tom hesitate at first?
Why was the evening special?
Did they enjoy the restaurant in the end?
answers_q: In the city center.
The Italian restaurant.
Because it was expensive.
Because it was one year since they started working together.
Yes, very much.
cloze: Tom thought the restaurant was too ______.
The waiter showed them to a ______.
speaking: You are choosing a restaurant with a friend.
example: “I think we should go to the Italian restaurant because the food is really good.”
grammar: Where do you want to eat?
I think we should go there.
Used to make suggestions and discuss plans with other people.

title: Two Apartments
content: Yesterday afternoon, I visited two apartments because I am planning to move soon. For several weeks, I had been searching online for a new place to live, but finding the right apartment was more difficult than I expected.
Some apartments were too expensive, others were too far from work, and a few looked completely different from the photos online.
Yesterday, however, I finally found two interesting options.

The first apartment was located near the city center, close to shops, cafés, and public transport. When I arrived, the building looked a little old from the outside, but the apartment itself was clean and bright.
“This apartment is small,” she explained, “but many people like the location.”
The apartment only had one bedroom and a small kitchen, but everything felt practical and comfortable enough for one person. The rent was also much cheaper than most apartments in the area.
Later that afternoon, I visited the second apartment.
It was bigger, more modern, and much more comfortable. The living room had large windows, the kitchen was beautiful, and there was even a small balcony with a nice view of the city.
“This place is amazing,” I thought while looking around.
For a few minutes, I imagined living there very easily.
The apartment was much more expensive.
The monthly rent was higher, and there were also extra costs for parking and building services. Even though I liked the apartment more, I immediately started worrying about my budget.
On the way home, I kept comparing the two places in my head.

The first apartment was smaller but affordable.
 The second apartment was bigger and more comfortable, but also more expensive.
For several hours, I was not sure what to choose.
I even made a small list of advantages and disadvantages for both apartments.
Finally, after thinking carefully, I realized something important: choosing an apartment is not only about comfort.
It is also about feeling financially safe and relaxed.
In the end, I decided to choose the cheaper apartment.
Of course, it was not perfect, and it was smaller than the second one. But the location was excellent, and the lower rent would allow me to save money and avoid stress later.
When I finally made the decision, I immediately felt calmer.
Sometimes the best choice is not the most impressive one.
Sometimes it is simply the most realistic decision for your life.
And honestly, I think I made the right choice.
questions: Why was the person visiting apartments?
What was good about the first apartment?
Why did the person like the second apartment?
What problem did the second apartment have?
Which apartment did the person choose?
answers_q: Because the person wanted to move.
It was cheaper and well located.
It was bigger and more comfortable.
It was too expensive.
The cheaper apartment.

In the end, the cheaper apartment was the better ______.

example: “One apartment was bigger, but the other one was cheaper and better for my budget.”
grammar: Used to compare places, prices, and situations.

title: Planning a Trip
content: Next month, I will travel to Spain with one of my close friends. We started planning the trip several weeks ago, and now we are both very excited about it.

For a long time, I wanted to visit Spain because I heard many good things about the country, the food, and the atmosphere. My friend visited Barcelona a few years ago and loved it, so this time we decided to discover Madrid together.
Almost every evening recently, we have been talking about the trip and organizing everything carefully.
“We really need to book the hotel this week,” my friend said during a phone call a few days ago.
“I know,” I replied. “I’m already looking at different options online.”
After comparing several hotels, we finally found one near the city center. It is not very luxurious, but the location is excellent because many museums, restaurants, and famous places are nearby.
That will make exploring the city much easier.

I am especially excited about visiting museums because I enjoy art and history very much. I already searched online for information about some famous museums in Madrid, and the pictures looked amazing.
One museum has paintings from very famous artists, and another one focuses on Spanish history.
“I definitely want to visit both,” I told my friend.
But the trip is not only about museums.
I also really want to try Spanish food.
Several people recommended traditional dishes like paella, tortilla española, and churros with chocolate. Honestly, just thinking about the food already makes me hungry.
My friend laughed when I said that.
“You’re planning the trip around food,” she joked.

We also plan to walk around the city, visit parks, take pictures, and spend time in small cafés. Since neither of us likes rushing too much during vacations, we decided not to make a very strict schedule.
“We’ll explore slowly and enjoy the atmosphere,” my friend said.
I think that is the best way to travel.
Recently, I also started practicing a few basic Spanish expressions online because I want to communicate more easily during the trip.
At the moment, I only know simple phrases like “hello,” “thank you,” and “where is the station?” but it is still fun to learn.
The closer the trip gets, the more excited I become.
Traveling to another country always feels special because you discover new places, different cultures, and new experiences.
I know there will probably be small problems or unexpected situations during the trip, but that is also part of traveling.
And honestly, I think this is going to be an unforgettable experience.
questions: Where will the person travel next month?
Who will travel with the person?
Why is the hotel location good?
What food does the person want to try?
How does the person feel about the trip?
answers_q: To Spain.
A friend.
Because it is near many famous places.
Paella, tortilla española, and churros.
Very excited.
cloze: The friends booked a ______ near the city center.
The trip will probably be very ______.
speaking: You are talking about a future trip.
example: “Next month, I will travel to Spain with a friend, and we will explore Madrid together.”
grammar: I am going to visit Madrid.
Used to talk about future plans and intentions.

title: Saying No Politely
content: Yesterday evening, one of my friends invited me to a party after work. Normally, I enjoy spending time with friends, especially on weekends, so at first I was interested.
The invitation arrived during the afternoon while I was still at the office.
“Hey,” my friend wrote in a message, “some people are meeting tonight for a party. Do you want to come?”
At first, I seriously thought about going.
I had not seen some of those friends for several weeks, and the party sounded fun. Everyone planned to meet at a bar in the city center before going to a friend’s apartment later in the evening.
But the more I thought about it, the more tired I realized I actually was.
The entire week had been stressful and busy. I slept badly several nights, worked late almost every day, and felt exhausted physically and mentally.
While sitting at my desk, I suddenly imagined myself staying home quietly instead of going out.
Honestly, that idea sounded much better.
Still, I felt slightly uncomfortable saying no because I did not want my friend to think I was ignoring him or refusing the invitation for a bad reason.
For several minutes, I looked at my phone without answering.
Finally, I decided to be honest and polite.
“I’m sorry,” I wrote, “I can’t come tonight. I’m really tired and I think I need some rest.”
A few minutes later, my friend replied.
“No problem at all,” he answered. “You should rest if you’re tired.”
Reading that immediately made me feel better.
Sometimes people worry too much about disappointing others, but real friends usually understand situations like that.
After work, I returned home slowly and changed into comfortable clothes. Instead of loud music and crowded rooms, I prepared tea, ordered food, and watched a movie from the sofa.
The apartment was quiet and peaceful.
For the first time all week, I finally felt calm.
At one point during the evening, my friend even sent me a funny picture from the party with the message:
I laughed when I saw it.
Of course, going out could have been enjoyable too. But yesterday, resting felt much more important than socializing.
Sometimes saying “no” politely is necessary, especially when your body and mind need a break.
And by the end of the evening, I knew I made the right decision.
questions: What invitation did the friend make?
Why did the person hesitate at first?
Why did the person refuse the invitation?
How did the friend react?
What did the person do at home?
answers_q: To go to a party.
Because the party sounded fun.
Because the person was very tired.
He understood and was kind.
Relaxed, drank tea, and watched a movie.

The person felt very ______ after work.
speaking: You are politely refusing an invitation.
example: “I’m sorry, but I can’t come tonight because I really need some rest.”
grammar: Used to refuse invitations politely and explain reasons

title: Returning a Product
content: Yesterday, I returned a jacket to a clothing store because there was a problem with the size. A few days earlier, I bought the jacket during a shopping trip in the city center.
At the store, everything seemed perfect.
The jacket looked stylish, the color was nice, and the material felt comfortable. I tried it on quickly in front of a mirror and thought it looked fine.
“I’ll take it,” I told the cashier confidently.
When I arrived home later that evening, I tried the jacket on again more carefully.
That was when I noticed the problem.

At first, I thought maybe it was just uncomfortable because I was tired after shopping. But after wearing it for a few more minutes, I realized the jacket was definitely too tight around the shoulders and arms.
I really liked the jacket, so I hoped the shop still had a bigger size available.

The next afternoon, I returned to the store carrying the shopping bag and receipt. Honestly, I felt a little nervous because I do not enjoy returning products. Sometimes customer service can be difficult.
Luckily, the store was calm and not too crowded.
I walked toward the cashier and smiled politely.
“Hello,” I said. “I bought this jacket yesterday, but the size is too small. Is it possible to exchange it?”
The cashier listened carefully and nodded.
“Of course,” she replied kindly. “Do you have the receipt?”
“Yes,” I answered while giving it to her.
She checked the jacket carefully to make sure everything was in good condition. Then she looked at the information on the computer.
“Good news,” she said after a moment. “We still have a bigger size.”
The cashier went to the back of the store and returned a few minutes later with another jacket.
“Would you like to try this one?” she asked.
I went to the fitting room and put it on.
This time, the jacket fit perfectly.
The shoulders felt comfortable, and I could move easily without feeling restricted.
“Yes,” I said happily when I returned. “This size is much better.”
A few minutes later, the exchange was finished. Before leaving, I thanked her for her help.
Outside the store, I looked at the shopping bag and smiled.
The situation started as a small problem, but everything was solved very easily in the end.
And honestly, good customer service can really change the entire shopping experience.
questions: What product did the person buy?
What was the problem with the jacket?
Why was the person nervous about returning it?
What did the cashier do?
How did the person feel at the end?
answers_q: A jacket.
It was too small.
Because customer service is sometimes difficult.
She exchanged the jacket for a bigger size.
Satisfied and relieved.
speaking: You are returning a product to a store.
example: “Hello, I bought this jacket yesterday, but the size is too small. Can I exchange it?”
grammar: She gave me a bigger one.
Used to describe shopping situations and product problems in the past.

title: My Favorite Place
content: My favorite place in the city is the park near my apartment. I go there almost every weekend, and sometimes I also visit it after work when I need to relax.
The park is not very famous or touristy, but for me it is one of the best places in the entire city.
I like it because it is quiet and peaceful.
During the week, the city often feels noisy and stressful. Cars, buses, people, and traffic are everywhere. After several busy days, I enjoy spending time somewhere calmer.
The park helps me slow down and relax.

There are many large trees, flowers, and walking paths. In spring and summer, everything becomes green and colorful. Birds sing in the trees, and people sit quietly on benches reading books or talking softly.
Sometimes, when the weather is good, I bring coffee and sit there for one hour doing almost nothing.
Honestly, those moments are some of my favorites.
I also enjoy walking in the park very much. There is a small path around a lake, and I often walk there while listening to music or podcasts.
The atmosphere always feels different from the rest of the city.
When I walk there, I think more clearly and feel less stressed.
Sometimes I meet friends at the park too.
On sunny weekends, we sit together on the grass, talk about work and life, and spend several hours relaxing outside. Occasionally, we bring snacks or drinks and stay there until the evening.
One of my friends once told me:
I laughed because it was true.
There is something special about the place.
Even during difficult or stressful weeks, visiting the park helps me feel calmer and more balanced again.
In autumn, the park becomes beautiful in a different way. The leaves change color and cover the ground in red, orange, and yellow. I love walking there during that season too.
Sometimes I simply sit on a bench and watch people passing by.
Families walk with children, people run or ride bikes, and couples talk quietly together.
The park feels alive, but never stressful.
Of course, there are bigger or more modern places in the city. But for me, this park is special because it always makes me feel relaxed and comfortable.
That is why I think it is the best place in the city.
And honestly, I hope it never changes.
questions: Why does the person like the park?
What does the person do there?
Who does the person sometimes meet in the park?
What does the park look like in autumn?
How does the park make the person feel?
answers_q: Because it is quiet and peaceful.
Walks, relaxes, and thinks.
Friends.
The leaves become red, orange, and yellow.
Relaxed and calm.

The park helps the person feel more ______.
speaking: You are describing your favorite place in your city.
example: “My favorite place is a quiet park near my apartment because it helps me relax.”
grammar: I like it because it is quiet.
I think it is the best place.
Used to describe preferences and personal opinions.

title: Inviting a Friend
content: Yesterday afternoon, I decided to call one of my friends because I wanted to go out in the evening instead of staying home alone.
The week had been long and stressful, and honestly I needed a break. After finishing work, I sat on the sofa for a few minutes thinking about what to do.
For a moment, I considered staying home quietly and watching a movie.
But then I thought, “Maybe it would be nicer to spend time with someone.”
So I picked up my phone and called my friend Daniel.
“A little tired from work,” he admitted. “But okay.”
We talked for a few minutes about our day, our jobs, and some small problems from the week. Then I finally asked:
“Do you want to go out tonight?”
Then Daniel laughed softly and said:
Immediately, I felt happy because I was afraid he might already have plans.
At first, we discussed several ideas. Daniel suggested a burger restaurant near the station, while I proposed a small Italian restaurant in the city center.
“The Italian place sounds better,” he admitted after thinking for a moment.
Then we decided to meet at 7 o’clock in front of the restaurant.
After the call ended, I suddenly felt much more relaxed and excited about the evening.
Sometimes, even a simple plan can improve your mood completely.
Later, after changing clothes and getting ready, I took the metro to the city center. The streets were lively because many people were already outside enjoying the evening.
When I arrived at the restaurant, Daniel was already waiting near the entrance checking his phone.
“Only by two minutes,” he replied with a smile.
Inside, the restaurant felt warm and comfortable. Soft music played quietly in the background, and the smell of pizza and pasta filled the room.
We ordered dinner, drinks, and dessert while talking for hours about work, travel, funny memories, and future plans.
At one point, we laughed so loudly that people at the next table looked at us and smiled.
Honestly, the evening felt simple but really enjoyable.
Sometimes people think special moments need big plans or expensive activities.
But often, good conversations and time with a friend are enough.
When I returned home later that night, I felt calmer, happier, and less stressed than before.
And I was very glad I decided to make that phone call.
questions: Why did the person call the friend?
Who did the person invite?
Which restaurant did they choose?
What did they do during dinner?
How did the person feel after the evening?
answers_q: Because the person wanted to go out.
Daniel.
An Italian restaurant.
They talked and laughed together.
Happier and more relaxed.
speaking: You are inviting a friend to go out.
example: “Hi! Do you want to go out for dinner tonight?”
grammar: Do you want to go out?
Used to invite people and organize social activities.

title: Choosing Between Two Jobs
content: A few weeks ago, I received two job offers at almost the same time. After several months of searching and attending interviews, I was very happy and relieved.
For a long time, I worried that I would not find the right opportunity.
And suddenly, I had to choose between two different jobs.
At first, the situation seemed exciting.
But after a few days, it also became stressful because both offers had advantages and disadvantages.
The first job offered a higher salary and better bonuses. The company was modern, well known, and the office looked impressive during the interview.
“This could be a great opportunity,” I thought.
However, there was one important problem.
The office was very far from my home.
According to the schedule, I would need almost two hours every day for transportation. That meant waking up earlier, returning home late, and spending a lot of time on trains and buses.
When I imagined that routine every day, I immediately felt tired.
The second job was completely different.
The salary was lower, and the office was smaller and less modern. But the company atmosphere felt relaxed and friendly during the interview.
Most importantly, the office was close to my apartment.
I could arrive there in only twenty minutes.
At first, I focused mainly on the salary difference. Of course, earning more money sounded attractive.
But little by little, I started thinking about other things too.
“What kind of daily life do I really want?” I asked myself.
I thought carefully about stress, free time, transportation, sleep, and work-life balance.
Some evenings, I even made lists comparing both jobs.

Higher salary versus shorter commute.
 Bigger company versus more free time.
 More pressure versus better balance.
The more I thought about it, the clearer the answer became.
I realized that free time and quality of life were becoming more important to me than earning the highest possible salary.

I wanted more energy after work.
 More time for friends and hobbies.
 And less stress every morning.
So finally, I chose the second job.
When I informed the company about my decision, I immediately felt calmer and more confident.
“Really?” one friend asked. “You refused the higher salary?”
“Yes,” I answered. “Because I think this job fits my life better.”
And honestly, I still believe it was the right decision.
Sometimes the best choice is not simply about money.
Sometimes it is about building a healthier and happier daily life.
questions: Why was the person happy at first?
What was good about the first job?
Why was the second job attractive?
What became more important to the person?
Which job did the person choose?
answers_q: Because the person received two job offers.
It paid more money.
It was closer to home and less stressful.
Free time and quality of life.
The second job.
cloze: The first job was ______ from home.
In the end, the second job was better for the person’s ______.
speaking: You are explaining why you chose one job over another.
example: “I chose the second job because it gives me more free time and less stress.”
grammar: It was better for my life.
Used to compare options and explain decisions.

title: A Change of Plans
content: Yesterday, I had plans to go out in the evening after work. One of my friends invited me to a small event in the city center, and at first I was really looking forward to it.
The week had been busy, so I thought it would be nice to spend time outside, see people, and relax a little.
During the afternoon, I checked the weather quickly on my phone, and everything still looked fine.
So after work, I returned home, changed clothes, and started getting ready.
But while I was preparing to leave, I suddenly heard rain hitting the windows.
I walked closer and looked outside.
Dark clouds covered the sky, heavy rain was falling everywhere, and strong wind moved the trees outside my apartment building.
For a few minutes, I hoped the rain would stop quickly. I even checked the weather app several times.
But the forecast became worse and worse.
The event was outdoors, and honestly, standing outside in heavy rain did not sound enjoyable at all.
So finally, I decided to change my plans.
Instead of going out, I stayed home.
At first, I felt a little disappointed because I was already prepared and excited for the evening. But after some time, I started enjoying the calm atmosphere inside the apartment.
I changed into comfortable clothes, prepared tea, and chose a movie to watch.
Outside, the rain continued falling heavily while I sat comfortably on the sofa under a blanket.
Surprisingly, the situation quickly became relaxing.
The movie was funny and entertaining, and the sound of the rain outside made the apartment feel even warmer and more peaceful.
Later in the evening, I became hungry and decided to cook dinner instead of ordering food. I made pasta with vegetables and listened to music while cooking.
The kitchen smelled wonderful, and I realized I was actually enjoying the evening much more than expected.
Sometimes we become frustrated when plans change suddenly.
But yesterday reminded me that unexpected changes are not always bad.
If the weather had stayed nice, I probably would have gone out and returned home tired very late.
Instead, I spent a calm evening resting, cooking, watching a movie, and relaxing properly.
By the end of the night, I felt peaceful and comfortable.
And honestly, I think staying home was exactly what I needed.
Sometimes change really is a good thing.
questions: What was the person’s original plan?
Why did the person change the plan?
How did the person feel at first?
What did the person do at home?
How did the evening end?
answers_q: To go out to an event.
Because it started raining heavily.
Disappointed.
Watched a movie, cooked dinner, and listened to music.
Calmly and happily.
cloze: In the end, the evening felt very ______.
speaking: You are explaining why you changed your plans.
example: “I wanted to go out, but it started raining, so I stayed home and relaxed instead.”
grammar: Used to describe changing plans and reacting to situations.

title: Why I Like My Job
content: People often complain about their jobs, but honestly, I like mine for several reasons. Of course, some days are stressful or tiring, like in every profession, but overall I feel happy and comfortable in my work environment.
The first reason is that the work itself is interesting.
Every day, I learn something new. Some tasks are simple, while others require more concentration and problem-solving. Because of that, the job never feels completely repetitive.
I enjoy discovering new ideas, improving my skills, and finding solutions to different situations.
Sometimes my manager gives me projects that are difficult at first, but I actually like that challenge.
“It helps you improve,” one colleague told me recently.
And I think that is true.
Another reason I enjoy my job is my colleagues.
The people I work with are friendly, respectful, and helpful. In many workplaces, the atmosphere can become stressful or competitive, but fortunately that is not really the case in my office.
When someone has a problem or too much work, other people usually try to help.
For example, last week I had difficulty finishing an important task before a deadline. One colleague noticed I was stressed and immediately offered assistance.
“Don’t worry,” she said. “We’ll finish it together.”
That kind of support makes a big difference.
Because of the positive atmosphere, going to work every morning feels easier and more pleasant.
We also talk during lunch breaks, laugh together sometimes, and help each other when necessary. Those small daily interactions make the workday much more enjoyable.
Another important reason is the flexible schedule.
In my job, I can organize some parts of my day more freely than in many other companies. Of course, there are still responsibilities and deadlines, but I also have enough time for my personal life.
That balance is very important to me.
After work, I still have time to relax, see friends, exercise, or simply rest at home. I do not feel completely exhausted every evening, which helps me stay happier and healthier overall.
Sometimes people think salary is the only important thing in a job.
But for me, the atmosphere, work-life balance, interesting tasks, and supportive colleagues are equally important.
When all those things are combined together, work becomes much more enjoyable.
That is why, despite occasional stressful days, I honestly feel grateful for my current job.
And for all these reasons, I truly enjoy what I do.
questions: Why does the person find the work interesting?
How are the colleagues at work?
What happened last week before a deadline?
Why is the flexible schedule important?
Does the person enjoy the job overall?
answers_q: Because the person learns new things every day.
Friendly and helpful.
A colleague helped with an important task.
Because it allows more personal time.
Yes, very much.
speaking: You are explaining why you like your job.
example: “I like my job because the work is interesting and my colleagues are very supportive.”
grammar: I like my job because it is interesting.
Used to explain opinions and give simple arguments.

title: Choosing a Movie
content: Last Saturday evening, Tom and Anna decided to spend the evening together at Anna’s apartment. Both of them were tired after a busy week, so they wanted a quiet and relaxing night.
Anna prepared popcorn and drinks while Tom looked through different movies on the television.
“There are so many options,” Tom said while scrolling through the streaming service.
Anna sat down on the sofa with a bowl of popcorn and smiled.
“What movie do you want to watch?” Tom asked.
“Because they are not interesting for me,” he explained. “Most of them are too predictable.”
“I think they are fun,” she answered. “Sometimes I just want to relax and laugh.”
Tom understood her point, but he still preferred different kinds of movies.
“Maybe,” he said, “but I prefer action movies.”
At first, they continued discussing different movie ideas. Anna suggested romantic comedies and funny family movies, while Tom proposed action films with car chases, mysteries, and explosions.
“That movie looks terrible,” Anna joked while looking at one of Tom’s suggestions.
Even though they disagreed, the conversation stayed friendly and relaxed. Neither of them became angry because they both understood that people simply have different tastes sometimes.
After several minutes, Anna smiled and said:
“Okay, let’s find something we both like.”
So together, they continued searching more carefully. Eventually, they found an adventure movie that included both funny moments and exciting action scenes.
“Yes,” Tom agreed. “It has both comedy and action.”
They started the movie while eating popcorn and drinking soda. Surprisingly, both of them enjoyed it very much.
Sometimes they laughed loudly during funny scenes, and other times they watched quietly during exciting moments.
At one point, Anna looked at Tom and smiled.
When the movie ended later that evening, both of them felt happy and relaxed.
The evening reminded them that disagreements are normal, and sometimes the best solution is simply finding a compromise that works for everyone.
questions: Why did Tom and Anna stay home?
What kind of movie did Anna want?
Why did Tom dislike comedies?
What solution did they find?
Did they enjoy the final movie?
answers_q: Because they wanted a relaxing evening.
A comedy.
Because he found them predictable.
They chose a movie with comedy and action.
Yes, both of them enjoyed it.
cloze: They decided to find something they both ______.
In the end, the movie was a good ______.
speaking: You are discussing movie preferences with a friend.
example: “I prefer action movies, but we can choose something we both enjoy.”
grammar: Let’s find something we both like.
Used to express preferences, opinions, and polite disagreement.

title: Living in the City
content: I live in a big city, and overall I really enjoy it. Of course, city life is not always perfect, and sometimes it can feel stressful or tiring. But for me, the advantages are stronger than the disadvantages.
One of the biggest advantages is the number of opportunities.
There are many jobs, companies, and professional events in the city. Because of that, it is usually easier to find work or meet new people professionally.
For example, when I was searching for a job last year, I found many interviews and opportunities very quickly.
In smaller towns, that can sometimes be more difficult.
Another reason I enjoy city life is that there are always things to do.
If I want to see a movie, visit a museum, try a new restaurant, or meet friends at a café, everything is nearby. There are concerts, festivals, shops, parks, and activities almost every weekend.
Sometimes, even simply walking through different neighborhoods feels interesting because every area has a different atmosphere.
I rarely feel bored living here.
Public transportation is also very practical. I do not need a car every day because buses, trains, and the metro connect most places in the city.
That makes daily life easier in many situations.
However, city life also has disadvantages.
The streets are often busy, especially during the morning and evening. Cars, buses, construction work, and people talking loudly can sometimes become exhausting.
On weekends, some neighborhoods stay noisy very late at night.
Another problem is the cost of living.
Apartments are expensive, restaurants cost more money, and daily expenses can become stressful sometimes. Many people work hard just to pay rent and bills.
Personally, I also miss nature occasionally.
Sometimes I want quieter places with fewer people, cleaner air, and slower days.
That is why I enjoy visiting smaller towns or parks from time to time.
Still, even with those problems, I like living in the city very much.
The energy, opportunities, and variety of activities make life feel dynamic and interesting. I enjoy meeting different people and discovering new places regularly.
For me, the advantages are stronger than the disadvantages.
Of course, everyone prefers a different lifestyle. Some people enjoy calm countryside life more, while others prefer busy cities.
But personally, I think city life fits my personality well.
And right now, I honestly cannot imagine living anywhere else.
questions: What advantages does the city offer?
Why is transportation practical?
What problems does the city have?
What does the person sometimes miss?
Does the person still enjoy city life?
answers_q: Jobs and many activities.
Because public transportation is good.
Noise and high costs.
Nature and quiet places.
Yes, very much.
cloze: Public transportation makes life easier every ______.
City life can sometimes feel very ______.
The person thinks the advantages are ______.
speaking: You are explaining why you like or dislike city life.
example: “I like living in the city because there are many activities and opportunities.”
grammar: Even with these problems, I like living here.
Used to express balanced opinions with positive and negative points.

title: A Difficult Conversation
content: Yesterday, I had a difficult conversation with my manager at work. For several days, I had been thinking about it constantly, but I was nervous and unsure about how to begin.
Recently, my workload had become very heavy.

I stayed late at the office almost every evening, answered emails during weekends, and felt more stressed every day. At first, I tried to manage everything alone because I did not want to complain or create problems.
But little by little, the situation became too difficult.
I started sleeping badly and feeling tired all the time. Sometimes I even felt anxious before going to work in the morning.
“This can’t continue like this,” I thought.
Finally, yesterday morning, I decided that I needed to speak honestly with my manager.
Before the meeting, I repeated possible sentences in my head several times.

“What if he thinks I’m weak?”
 “What if he becomes angry?”
 “What if nothing changes?”
Those thoughts made me even more stressed.
Around midday, I knocked on my manager’s office door.
I sat down carefully, and for a few seconds I did not know how to start the conversation.
Then I took a deep breath and explained my situation honestly.

I told him that I enjoyed my work and wanted to do a good job, but recently the amount of work had become difficult to manage. I also explained that I felt exhausted and worried about becoming too stressed.
To my surprise, my manager listened very carefully without interrupting me.
He nodded several times while I spoke.
When I finished explaining, he stayed quiet for a moment before answering.
“Thank you for telling me,” he said calmly. “I didn’t realize the situation was becoming so difficult for you.”
Then we started discussing possible solutions together. We reviewed my current projects, talked about priorities, and decided to move some tasks to another team member temporarily.
He also encouraged me to take proper breaks and stop working late every evening.
“You don’t need to do everything alone,” he said.
Hearing that honestly changed how I felt immediately.
After the conversation ended, I returned to my desk feeling much lighter than before.
The situation was not magically perfect, but now I knew I was no longer carrying the problem alone.
Most importantly, I felt proud of myself.
Speaking honestly about difficult emotions is never easy, especially at work.
But yesterday taught me something important: sometimes difficult conversations are necessary to improve situations.
And often, they go much better than we expect.
questions: Why did the person want to speak with the manager?
How did the person feel before the conversation?
What problems did the person explain?
How did the manager react?
How did the person feel afterward?
answers_q: Because the workload was too heavy.
Nervous and stressed.
Too much work and exhaustion.
He listened carefully and helped.
Relieved and proud.
cloze: The person felt very ______ before the meeting.
After the conversation, the person felt much ______.
speaking: You are explaining a problem to your manager.
example: “I enjoy my work, but recently I have felt very stressed and tired.”
grammar: Used to describe emotions, problems, and discussions.

title: Buying a Laptop
content: A few weeks ago, I decided to buy a new laptop because my old one was becoming very difficult to use. At first, I tried to continue working with it, but every week new problems appeared.

Sometimes it became extremely slow.
 Other times, the battery stopped working after only thirty minutes.
 And occasionally, the screen froze completely while I was working.
One evening, after losing an important document because the laptop suddenly restarted, I finally said:
